Workaround: Download one of the attached .zip files, selecting one with a name corresponding to your version of MATLAB.Save the file in your matlabroot/java folder (matlabroot is the location on your file system returned by running the matlabroot function in MATLAB).Unzip the file using the MATLAB unzip function.Restart MATLAB. Note: the attached .zip files will only work with the version of MATLAB indicated by the file name.

Summary: Build process fails when S-function uses nonfinite parameter with column vector shape
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail for a model with a noninlined S-function that uses a parameter which: Has nonfinite values.  The shape of the parameter is a column vector (for example, 4 rows, 1 column).

Summary: imfinfo consumes too much memory on TIFFs with certain tags
Description: Certain TIFF tags can contain a great deal of information, sometimes ranging into the megabyte range. imfinfo  can consume too much memory reading in the metadata for these files, leading to an 'Out of Memory'  error.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the versions of these files in your MATLAB installation with the ones attached, following this procedure:Type matlabroot and mexext at the MATLAB prompt, noting the name of your MATLAB installation folder and the MEX file extension for your platform.Quit MATLAB.Download the attachment appropriate for your MATLAB release and unzip it into a temporary folder.Change folders into $matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/private, where $matlabroot is the name returned by your call to the matlabroot function.Backup the file tifftagsread.mex$mexext to tifftagsread.mex$mexext.bak. If your version of MATLAB is R2008a, you also need to make backup copies of imtifinfo.m and processtifftags.m as well.Copy the tifftagsread.mex$mexext MEX-file from the temporary folder into the current folder. If your release is R2008a, you must also copy the imtifinfo.m and processtifftags.m into the current folder.Restart MATLAB.Type rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Under rare circumstances, Stateflow wrongly removes calls to functions with side-effects
Description: Under rare circumstances, Stateflow wrongly removes calls to graphical functions that have side-effects. The attached model bug_g544880.mdl demonstrates this problem. The correct output generated by the chart is an increasing ramp. However, when debugging is turned off or when Real-Time Workshop code is generated for this model, the output is incorrectly computed as a constant zero. This problem occurs only when the following conditions are satisfied: The only side-effect of the graphical function is to write to a machine-parented data or a custom code symbol. If the graphical function has other side-effects, such as writing to a chart (or state) level data object, the bug does not occur. The graphical function has no output data or even if it does, the function is called without assigning the output to any variable. For example, the bug occurs if the action language says "foo()" but does not happen if we call the function as "y = foo()". The Function Inline Option  for the graphical function is set to  Function .
Workaround: If you are using R2009aThe following fix requires write access to the MATLAB installation area.  Use this fix only if you are using the R2009a version of MATLAB. See below for the workaround to use for previous versions.At the MATLAB prompt, type:which private/sfinit.mThat command should point to a file such as:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\toolbox\stateflow\stateflow\private\sfinit.mMake a backup copy of this file to a location outside the MATLAB installation area.Copy the attached sfinit.m to the location from step 1.If you are using a release prior to R2009aStarting from R2006b, when this bug was first introduced into the product, you can just type the following at the MATLAB prompt before generating code:sf('feature', 'Side-effect analysis', 0)However, this setting remains in effect only for a single MATLAB session. In order for this setting to be permanent across multiple sessions, you need to modify a shipping file. Follow these steps:At the MATLAB prompt, type:edit private/sfinitThat command should open an installation file such as:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008a\toolbox\stateflow\stateflow\private\sfinit.mBelow approximately line 16 of the above file, where it says sfinit('load defaults');add the line: sf('feature', 'Side-effect analysis', 0);This modification ensures that every time Stateflow is loaded, this feature is turned off appropriately.

Summary: xPC Target multirate models cannot detect CPU overload conditions when driven by external interrupts
Description: xPC Target multirate models cannot detect CPU overload conditions when driven by external interrupts.
Workaround: As a workaround, change the multirate model to a single rate one.?

Summary: Wrong assignment of tunable 'SimulinkGlobal' parameters in RSIM target.
Description: During the generation of an RSIM target, if tunable 'SimulinkGlobal' parameters are read from a parameter-structure MAT file, then they are incorrectly assigned in the target.
Workaround: One simple workaround is to change all of the 'SimulinkGlobal' parameters to 'ExportedGlobal' parameters.

Summary: classregtree objects saved in R2008b or before do not load properly in R2009a.
Description: If you create a decision tree by using the classregtree  function and save it as a .mat  file in MATLAB release R2008b or earlier, and then load in R2009a, the object does not function properly. Many methods produce an error message when you try to use them.

Summary: AXIS SQUARE causes misalignment of labels on boxplots.
Description: The boxplot  function draws labels for the boxes by placing text strings along the x or y axis. When the axis position changes, the text labels adjust their positions to remain aligned with the boxes. However, when the AXIS SQUARE  command changes the position of the axes, the text labels do not adjust properly and are no longer aligned with the boxes.
Workaround: Avoid using AXIS SQUARE, and instead adjust the position of the boxplot axes directly.

Summary: Passing HTML options to cvhtml generates an error.
Description: You might see an error in cvhtml if you call the function with the following arguments: cvhtml('filename', cpvdata, '-aTS=0 -bRG=0 -bTC=0 -hTR=0 -nFC=0');
Workaround: none

Summary: Error analyzing Modified Condition/Decision Coverage for very large expression
Description: Running a coverage simulation with the MCDC metric enabled on a model containing a block or model object representing a very large logical expression can cause an error or crash. For example, a Logical Operator block with many inputs or a Stateflow conditional transition containing a complex logical expression with many predicates could be affected by this bug. The error or crash might mention the following assertion failure:? (mcdc->pathStorageCount << 5) >= mcdc->pathCount  Depending on the context in which the coverage analysis was initiated, different error messages are shown.

Summary: PolySpace Queue Manager (Spooler) displays the error message 'no queue manager found'
Description: After successfully launching a server verification, the error 'no queue manager found' appears when you open the PolySpace Queue Manager (Spooler). The PolySpace Queue Manager reads the configuration file PolySpace-Launcher.prf  from the %USERPROFILE%  where the server address is saved. However, PolySpace Launcher cannot read the %USERPROFILE%  environment variable, and saves the configuration file in another location. The correct location of the configuration file is saved in the registry key 'HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell Folders\Desktop '
Workaround: As long as the address of the PolySpace server does not change, copying the PolySpace-Launcher.prf configuration file to the %USERPROFILE\.rte subfolder will workaround the issue.

Summary: Interaction between "end" and nested functions causes a crash.
Description: The following statement may cause a crash if used in a nested function or in a function that contains nested functions:     out = myvar(end).new;  If no nested functions are present, the statement executes correctly.
Workaround: To work around the problem, avoid the use of "end" in this context. For example:    out = myvar(length(myvar)).new

Summary: Exception displays in the Command Window after sorting on the Description column in the Current Directory browser
Description: In the Current Directory browser, when you show the Description column and then click the column header to sort on it, the following exception might appear in the Command Window. This happens when the current directory contains files that do not have an .m or .mdl extension. Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.mlwidgets.explorer.DocumentTableColumns$11.compare(DocumentTableColumns.java:204)
Workaround: Sort by any column except Description.

Summary: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 MATLAB? unexpectedly terminates when saving a data tip update function from the Callback Editor window
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms, MATLAB? unexpectedly exits when saving changes to data tip update functions using the Callback Editor. The editor is a window that displays code when you create a data tip in an axes, right-click it, and select Edit Text Update Function . MATLAB exits without warning when you click the Save  button in the editor.

Summary: PolySpace Queue Manager server stops and deletes the file 'queue-infos.txt' when hard-disk is full
Description: When your hard-disk is full, the Queue Manager clears the following file: '/var/PolySpace/queue-infos.txt ' (Linux workstation) 'c:\PolySpace\PolySpace_RLDatas\queue-infos.txt ' (Windows workstation)  This makes it impossible to access existing analysis files through the Queue Manager spooler. In addition, after the 'queue-infos.txt ' file is deleted, cleaning up the hard drive does not help anymore. ?

Summary: Writing to the output stream during library initialization, when standard out is not available or when standard out has been supplanted by an output handle, causes error
Description: This problem manifests itself in various use cases, including: Standalone application built with the -e  flag (WinMain target) .NET winform application using a .NET assembly generated by MATLAB Builder NE Microsoft Excel using a COM component (MATLAB Builder NE) or MATLAB Builder EX add-in Application using InitializeWithHandlers  to provide its own output handler function  The first output doesn't throw an error, but the second write operation prints the following to the log file: Error writing to output stream.  {??? Error using ==> displayError writing to output stream.  Error in ==> two_prints at 3}iolib:badbit  ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, download and unzip the attached file to \\MATLABROOT\bin\win32 or \\MCRROOT\bin\win32.

Summary: Java OutOfMemoryError displays in Command Window when hovering the mouse pointer over a large dataset variable in the Editor
Description: A Java OutOfMemoryError  may appear in the Command Window when you hover the mouse pointer over a large dataset variable in the Editor and either of the following is true: You are using edit mode and you previously enabled data tips (by selecting File > Preferences > Editor/Debugger > Display , and then choosing Enable datatips in edit mode ). You are using debug mode.
Workaround: Either do not use data tips with large dataset variables or do the following:Extract datatipinfo.m from the attached zip file.Select File > Set Path to open the Set Path dialog box.Add the folder containing the datatipinfo.m file that you downloaded ahead of the following folder, where $matlabroot is the folder returned when you type matlabroot in the Command Window: $matlabrooot\toolbox\matlab\codetools.Although the attached version of datatipinfo.m has been tested with R2008b and R2009a only, it may also work with older releases.

Summary: C MEX S-Function generated with Legacy Code Tool produces error when using Simulink.Bus objects with boolean bus elements.
Description: If you use the Legacy Code Tool to integrate legacy C functions, the generated C MEX S-Function causes a spurious error message during simulation. This error occurs under the following conditions: You use the Legacy Code Tool to integrate legacy C functions. The legacy C function argument is a structure data type. The function argument is defined in the base workspace using a Simulink.Bus object with bus elements as boolean .  For example, consider the following Legacy Code Tool function specification where aStruct  is: Defined as a structure data type in a legacy code header file Used as a function argument Implemented as a Simulink.Bus object in the base workspace  def.OutputFcnSpec = 'void foo(aStruct u1[1], aStruct y1[1])';  e = Simulink.BusElement  e.Name = 'field1';  e.DataType = 'boolean';  aStruct = Simulink.Bus;  aStruct.Elements = e;  In the generated C MEX S-Function, the structure field aStruct.field1  is a uint32  instead of a boolean .

Summary: Verification stops with message "Error: unable to fork/spawn" when there are a large number of -I entries in the launching command
Description: The verification stops at the beginning of the sources C verification phase with the following message: **************************************************************** Verifying C sources*************************************************************Error: unable to fork/spawn.  Please check your memory and swap amount.  This problem occurs when there are more than a few hundred -I  entries in the launching command.
Workaround: When possible, reducing number of -I allows to workaround the issue.

Summary: Help browser can freeze when documentation provided by other users is present??
Description: If you use documentation provided by other users in the Help browser, the Help browser sometimes becomes unresponsive.
Workaround: Use the Path browser to identify the info.xml file for the user-supplied documentation. Remove the folder containing that file from the search path, and restart MATLAB. Removing the folder from the search path also removes the user-supplied documentation from the Help browser.?

Summary: For some values, the hypergeom function of type 2F0 returns wrong results
Description: In both MATLAB workspace and MuPAD Notebook, hypergeom([a1, a2], [], x)  returns wrong floating point values for integer values a1 >= 8 (or a2 >= 8) and negative x. For example, using the MuPAD notebook interface: hypergeom([9, 0.2], [], -0.25) returns 0.005175740178 The correct result is 0.794939216
Workaround: Shift the integer parameters by a tiny amount, for example:scale:= 1 + 10^(-DIGITS);hypergeom([9*scale, 0.2], [], -0.25)produces the correct result0.794939216

Summary: Solving a system of differential equations with initial conditions vector of incorrect dimensions throws an error message with a mistake in it.
Description: When solving a system of differential equations, the length of the initial conditions vector must match the length of the vector returned by the derivative function. If these vectors are of different dimensions, the ODE solver throws an error. The error message returns the actual size of initial conditions vector, rather than the desired size. For example: myode = @(t,y) ones(3,1); % expected vector of length 3y0 = zeros(2,1); % supplied vector of length 2 ode45(myode,[0,1],y0); ??? Error using ==> odearguments at 116Solving @(T,Y)ONES(3,1) requires an initial condition vector of length 2. Error in ==> ode45 at 173[neq, tspan, ntspan, next, t0, tfinal, tdir, y0, f0, odeArgs, odeFcn, ...
Workaround: The error message correctly indicates a discrepancy between the dimensions of the initial conditions vector and the vector returned by the derivative function. This issue needs to be resolved before the ODE solver can be applied.

Summary: isequal does not treat object property values that belong to different numeric classes as equal.
Description: Given two values that belong to different numeric classes, isequal  returns true if the numeric values are equal. However, given two objects whose property values contain numeric values from different classes, isequal  returns false even if the numeric values are mathematically equal.

Summary: In Accelerator simulation mode, the Data Store Memory diagnostics may give an incorrect block path
Description: If a block resides in a code reuse subsystem and reads from or writes to a data store, and if the simulation mode for the model is Accelerator, then the Data Store Memory diagnostics may report an incorrect block path for the block. For example, consider the model 'mbug'. It contains subsystems Write_to_A_1 and Write_to_A_2, each of which contains a Data Store Write block that writes to the same Data Store Memory block A. They also reuse the same reusable function for code generation. Simulink may report the following incorrect error message for the Data Store Write block in Write_to_A_2: The block 'mbug/Write_to_A_1/Data Store Write' is writing to the data store 'mbug/Data Store Memory' but the block 'mbug/Write_to_A_1/Data Store Write' has already written to this memory at time 0. Whereas the correct message is: The block 'mbug/Write_to_A_2/Data Store Write' is writing to the data store 'mbug/Data Store Memory' but the block 'mbug/Write_to_A_1/Data Store Write' has already written to this memory at time 0.  ?

Summary: Dialog box for Stateflow data does not show an option for setting 'Complexity' in R2009a
Description: Support for complex data in Stateflow charts was added in the R2008a release. However, in R2009a, the dialog box for Stateflow data does not show an option to control the complexity of the data object. This bug affects only the user interface and exists only in the R2009a version. Versions R2008a and R2008b show the 'Complexity' option in the dialog box for Stateflow data.
Workaround: To fix this user-interface bug, follow these steps. (These steps require write access to the MATLAB installation area.)Download attached dataddg.m file.At the MATLAB prompt, type: which private/dataddgBack up the file to which the above command points. For example, rename the file to dataddg.m.backup and save it to a location that is not on the MATLAB path.Save the attached dataddg.m file at the location pointed to by the above command.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: ROOTMUSIC errors out when the second input argument is a 2-element vector.
Description: When invoking the ROOTMUSIC  function with the syntax W = ROOTMUSIC(X,P) , the function errors out if P  is a 2-element vector.

Summary: Setting map limits works improperly for the Wiechel projection
Description: An internal problem introduced in R2008b causes the Wiechel projection, which is azimuthal, to sometimes be treated as if it were a non-azimuthal projection. This can cause the map frame to either appear smaller than it should, or to have a "wavy" quadrangular shape.

Summary: Setting breakpoints sometimes results in "Cannot enable breakpoint(s) because of a syntax error" message
Description: Setting breakpoints in class definition files (or methods) that are not on the MATLAB? path can sometimes lead to an error dialog box that states, "Cannot enable breakpoint(s) because of a syntax error," followed by another message. The following two cases describe the conditions in which this occurs: Case 1: class files in "@" directories  Suppose pwd is C:\Temp\@trial and the file in the Editor has the path: C:\Temp\@trial\myfile.m. If you set a breakpoint in myfile.m using the Editor, it results in an error message stating "Cannot enable breakpoint(s) because of a syntax error," followed by "Cannot find function C:\Temp\@trial\myfile.m." Case 2: class files in package directories  Suppose pwd is C:\Temp\+testpackage\@trial and the file in the Editor has the path: C:\Temp\+testpackage\@trial\trial.m. If you set a breakpoint in this file from the Editor, it results in an error message stating, "Cannot enable breakpoint(s) because of a syntax error," followed by "The parent directory of class "testpackage.trial" must be on the MATLAB? search path."
Workaround: In both cases the workaround is the same. Do one of the following:Set pwd to a location other than inside the package or class directory.Add the parent folder of the package or class to the MATLAB path.

Summary: Incremental build might fail with "undefined reference" link error when referenced model requires additional library
Description: Building a model might generate a link error if the following conditions exist: The model contains a model reference hierarchy. At least one referenced model specifies additional libraries using a target function library (TFL) and/or an rtwmakecfg.m  file. The build is an incremental build.  During an incremental build of the model, the build process does not propagate the additional libraries up to the top model and "undefined reference " link errors might occur.
Workaround: To work around this bug, take one of the following actions:Delete the slprj folder and always perform full builds of the model.To restore the ability to do incremental builds of the model, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name:matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.mDownload the attached file and place it at the same location as the file you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: NCO block in a feedback loop can cause model update diagram to fail
Description: A model containing a Signal Processing Blockset NCO block in a feedback loop can fail model update diagram (Ctrl+D ) and display the following error: S-function method mdlSetInputPortDimensionInfo cannot change the dimension of output port 1 of <Block in model> once it has been set. This error only occurs if either the phase increment source or phase offset source comes from an input port.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Insert a Simulink Signal Specification block on the signal line that connects to the NCO block input port.Set the Dimensions parameter of the Signal Specification block to be the desired input dimension of the NCO block.If the NCO block has two input ports, connect a Signal Specification block to one of the two NCO inputs. If necessary, you can insert a Signal Specification block before both inputs.

Summary: Invalid behavior of ERT S-function generated for an AUTOSAR subsystem with an Explicit Receive Error Status input
Description: Real-Time Workshop may generate invalid code for the ERT S-function of an AUTOSAR subsystem that has an Explicit Receive Error Status input. As a consequence, the S-function may not behave correctly. You may not be able to validate the functionality of the target code generated using this S-function.

Summary: Generated code for Prelookup block can give wrong results for evenly-spaced, non-tunable breakpoints
Description: Generated code for the Prelookup block can give wrong numerical results when: The block has evenly-spaced, non-tunable breakpoints. The stored integer of the spacing is 2. The input data type is different from the breakpoint data type.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop S-function target: Building atomic subsystem with Minimize algebraic loop occurrences selected gets "undeclared identifier 'tid'" error
Description: Building a model might generate the compiler error "undeclared identifier 'tid' " if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains an atomic subsystem for which Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  is selected. The atomic subsystem has continuous states and discrete blocks. The Real-Time Workshop S-function target (rtwsfcn.tlc ) is selected for the model.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2007b, R2008a, R2008b, or R2009a, you can either clear the subsystem option Minimize algebraic loop occurrences for the parent subsystem, or download one of the attached files.To download the appropriate file, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/rtmdllib.tlcDownload the attached file rtmdllib_suffix.tlc, where suffix corresponds to the last two characters of your MATLAB release number, and place the file at the same location as the file you renamed. Rename the file to remove the suffix. For example, for R2007b, download the file rtmdllib_7b.tlc and rename it to rtmdllib.tlc.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: The Show propagated signals setting for an Inport block's output signal may revert to 'off'
Description: The Show propagated signals  setting for the signal that is output by an Inport block may revert to 'off'  in the following circumstances: The Inport block is within a subsystem, as distinct from being a top-level root Inport. A bus signal is connected to the subsystem input port that corresponds to the Inport block. A bus object is specified in the Signal Attributes  pane of the Inport's Block Parameters dialog. Show propagated signals  is set to 'on'  in the Signal properties  dialog for the Inport's output signal.  In this case, when the model is saved, closed, and reopened, Show propagated signals  reverts to 'off' .

Summary: User-specified sample time priority not honored in generated code for multirate model
Description: Sample time priority assignments in the generated code for a multirate model might be incorrect if both of the following conditions are true: You use the model configuration parameter Sample time properties  to assign priorities to the sample times implemented by your model. One or more discrete periodic sample times specified in Sample time properties  does not exist in the model.  For example, suppose that you specify Sample time properties , using the [period offset priority ]  format, as follows: [[0.1 0 10][0.2 0 20][0.4 0 40][1.2 0.3 50]] If the model contains sample times [0.1 0.0] , [0.2 0.0] , and [1.2 0.3] , but does not  contain sample time [0.4 0.0] , priority 40 is incorrectly assigned to sample time [1.2 0.3] .
Workaround: To work around this bug, make sure that Sample time properties specifies only sample times present in the model.

Summary: While a simulation is running, autoscaling data in the Discrete-Time Eye Diagram Scope block causes an error.
Description: While a simulation is running, autoscaling data in the Discrete-Time Eye Diagram Scope block causes an error.  You can autoscale the data by selecting either the 'Autoscale' option under the Axes menu or the 'Autoscale' option in the axes context menu in the scope figure window.The error message is:Reference to non-existent field 'zoomInUse'

Summary: On Solaris systems, the command "psqueue-progress -open-launcher <analysis #>" fails with no message
Description: On Solaris systems, you can follow verification status by using the command 'psqueue-progress -open-launcher <analysis #>". However, in some cases this command may fail (nothing happens - no gui opens, and there is no message).
Workaround: To workaround this issue, remove the libraries libthread.so.1 and libthread.so from the folder: <PolySpace_Directory>/PolySpace_Common/RemoteLauncher/lib.

Summary: Function prototype for an AUTOSAR periodic runnable has an argument when model is multirate.
Description: The function prototype for an AUTOSAR periodic runnable contains the argument "tid" when the model is multirate. However, this prototype must not have any arguments.
Workaround: Download the attached files and use these to overwrite the existing files. Ensure that you install the correct version in the following locations:/matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/autosar.tlc/matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/setuplib.tlc

Summary: Assignment block triggers seg-v due to out-of-range indices
Description: The Assignment block triggers a seg-v during model compilation when the following conditions hold: On the Function Block Parameters dialog, the "Index Option" is set to either "Index vector (port)" or "Starting index (port)" for at least one dimension. The parameter, "Initialize output (Y)", is set to "Specify size for each dimension in table". The index of the last element assigned, based on the signal dimension at input port "U", is out of range of the output "Y".

Summary: The nitfread function leaves file handles open
Description: The nitfread  function leaves a handle to the NITF file open. This might make it impossible to rename, move, or delete the file without quitting MATLAB. Issuing the command fclose('all')  does not close this file handle.
Workaround: Make a backup copy of the rnitfc MEX-file in the matlabroot/toolbox/images/iptformats/private directory, where matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: rnitfc.m.oldDownload the attachment and extract the version of the rnitfc MEX-file for your platform(s) and store it in the directory specified in the previous step. (32-bit Windows = .mexw32, 32-bit Linux = .mexglx, 64-bit Windows = .mexw64, 32-bit Mac = .mexmaci, 64-bit Mac = .mexmaci64, 64-bit Linux = .mexa64, 64-bit Solaris = .mexs64)Copy the downloaded file into the iptformats/private directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: MATLAB Builder NE WebFigures fails in IIS when Anonymous access is turned off.
Description: When using MATLAB Builder NE WebFigures in IIS, and working in a virtual directory where Integrated Windows Authentication is turned on and Anonymous access is turned off, an error is displays indicating that the WebFigure Service is unavailable. This is caused by WebFigure Service not supporting Windows Authentication.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, either:Enable Anonymous access for this virtual directory orDeploy the WebFigure in a virtual directory that has Anonymous access enabled. Use the remote invocation features of WebFigures from the main website that has Windows Authentication enabled. See Advanced Configuration options in the MATLAB Builder NE WebFigures documentation for more information.

Summary: Reading large tiled JPEG2000 image with imread causes segmentation violation (crash)
Description: Using imread  to read large JPEG2000 images containing tiles (e.g. 1024 x 1024 tiles) causes a segmentation violation (crash) in MATLAB.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download this updated file (attached) and put it into your MATLAB installation folder, following this procedure:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following files     $MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/private/readjp2c.mex$MEXEXT$MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/private/readjp2.mwhere $MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation folder, as returned by the matlabroot function, and where $MEXEXT represents the file extension for your platform, as returned by the mexext function. For example, on a 64-bit linux machine, copy readjp2c.mexa64 and readjp2.m to readjp2c.mexa64.old and readjp2.m.old respectively.Download and unzip the attached zip archive file corresponding to the MATLAB release you have installed. Download readjp2_r2009a.zip for MATLAB Release 2009a or readjp2_r2008b.zip for MATLAB Release 2008b.Copy the file that is specific to your platform (also identified by mexext) into the folders specified in the step 2. Please note that JPEG2000 support is not available on Solaris systems.Restart MATLAB.Run the     rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For large signals and blocks not included in the Simulink block library
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for models containing large signals and blocks not in the Simulink block library. For example, custom driver blocks, custom look up table blocks, or blocks from an embedded target library.
Workaround: Download the attached zip archive and extract the p-file. Execute the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:>> setRTWCGGlobals2Locals(0)

Summary: solve may error if the system you solve involves polynomials with RootOf solutions.
Description: A call to solve  with a system involving RootOf  solutions may error: syms x y z h = x^3 + y^3 - z^3 - x*y*z + x - 10*y^2; hx = diff(h,x); hy = diff(h,y); hz = diff(h,z); [xcrit,ycrit,zcrit] = solve(hx,hy,hz) returns ??? Error using ==> mupadengine.mupadengine>mupadengine.feval at 162 Error: types of matrices don't match [linalg::stackMatrix] Error in ==> solve>mupadSolve at 211 list = feval(symengine,'mlsolve',eqns,vars);
Workaround: To get the complete MuPAD solution involving RootOf expressions, use the command:res = feval(symengine, 'solve', [hx hy hz])

Summary: Real-Time Workshop code generation of a model using an AUTOSAR target might fail
Description: Real-Time Workshop code generation might fail for a model using an AUTOSAR target. The following error appears in the MATLAB command window: CGIR assertion in .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_vcat_scal.cpp(1191): 'isPointerToMatrixType(nodeT)'

Summary: The Biquad Filter block can produce incorrect simulation results
Description: The Biquad Filter block can produce incorrect simulation results in either of the following two cases: The fraction length of the numerator accumulator data type is not the same as the fraction length of the denominator accumulator data type. The fraction length of the numerator product output data type is not the same as the fraction length of the denominator product output data type.  Simulation errors can appear if the output data type has similar numeric resolution to the accumulator or product output data types. This is not observed in most cases because the output data type is usually set at a lower numeric resolution than the accumulator and product output data types. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, use an identical fraction length for the numerator and denominator accumulator data types, and/or use an identical fraction length for the numerator and denominator product output data types.

Summary: License Manager Error occurs when using mcc from Unix Shell on Solaris 10
Description: Customer is running a network license. While using MATLAB Compiler from the Unix shell with Solaris 10 clients running 7.8, they get the following: Could not check out a Compiler license: Cannot read data from license server system. The license server system appears to be running, but is not responding. If this persists, notify the System Administrator. The license server manager (lmgrd ) and vendor daemon processes should be terminated and restarted.) Feature: Compiler Hostname: hostname License path: /path FLEXnet Licensing error:-16,287 For further information, refer to the FLEXnet Licensing documentation, available at "www.acresso.com".. This issue may happen on 32 and 64bit Linux system as well.
Workaround: Run mcc from within MATLAB.

Summary: Using empty (zero-length) strings in a state may cause errors.
Description: When you use empty (zero-length) strings legally in state labels, errors occur if you turn on syntax highlighting. For example, if you call a function in a state action, and pass that function an empty string -- such as ml.foo(2,'')  -- syntax highlighting stops working, malformed strings appear on your screen, and errors are written to the console. Empty strings are legal only when passed to external C-code functions or MATLAB functions as arguments.
Workaround: Turn off syntax highlighting for models that require legal uses of empty strings.

Summary: Reference line for goodness of fit plot showing observed versus predicted estimates incorrect in some cases
Description: A goodness of fit plot displays observed versus predicted estimates against a y = x line. If the axes limits are not square, meaning that the lengths of the X and Y axes are different, and/or the offsets (i.e. X min and Y min) are different, then the line plotted will not be y = x.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Generated code for instance of reusable subsystem with asynchronous sample time can cause incorrect results
Description: The build process might generate incorrect code for an instance of a reusable subsystem if all of the following conditions are true: Your model contains at least two instances of the reusable subsystem. The instances are both single-rate, but have different rates. At least one of the instances has an asynchronous sample time.  Under these conditions, the code generation software incorrectly emits the code body of the reusable subsystem after calling its function. This is unnecessary and redundant because the code for the body of the single-rate reusable subsystem is constructed separately. The incorrect code potentially can cause compilation errors or lead to incorrect results during execution.
Workaround: To work around this bug, locate all atomic subsystems running at the asynchronous rate in the model, and for each one, change the generated code format to Inline or Function using one of the following MATLAB commands:set_param(block,'RTWSystemCode','Inline');set_param(block,'RTWsystemCode','Function');For block, specify either the full path name of the subsystem block in single quotes or, if the block is selected, gcb.

Summary: Nested block comments cause errors in MATLAB Compiler-generated applications and shared libraries.
Description: The use of nested block comments in MATLAB files causes errors when these files are used in applications or shared libraries generated by MATLAB Compiler or any of the Builder products. For example, this function will report an error about an unterminated string constant when compiled: function my_broken_function%{ This is a comment%{This is still a comment%}This should be a comment, but isn't%}disp('You''ll never see this message'); When compiled, this function issues the error: ??? Error: File: my_broken_function.mLine: 4 Column: 31A MATLAB string constant is not terminated properly. MATLAB:m_unterminated_string This indicates the final comment is being interpreted by MATLAB as code, rather than being ignored.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, do not use nested block comments in your MATLAB files.

Summary: MATLAB displays the error "It is not possible to assign a value to an externally defined structure" when using LOADLIBRARY and CALLLIB?
Description: When using LOADLIBRARY  and CALLLIB  to write to or create a structure containing another structure with pointers, MATLAB displays the error: ??? Error using ==> fevalIt is not possible to assign a value to an externally defined structure.
Workaround: The attached files patch the R2009a release. To install the patch files:Locate your MATLAB install directory, which can be found by typing MATLABROOT from the MATLAB command window.Locate the files libmwcli.* in your matlabroot/bin/arch directory, where arch corresponds to your platform, and rename to libmwcli.orig.Download the attached file and extract the files for your platform to the directory in step 2.?

Summary: Using a builtin function with the same case-insensitive name as a private function can cause a crash.
Description: Using a builtin function with the same case-insensitive name as a private function can cause a crash, e.g. isempty and IsEmpty. Example reproduction steps: (1) Create a function   function r = testEmpty     x='aaa';     r = isempty(x);   end (2) Create a private function ./private/IsEmpty.m    function r = IsEmpty(x)       disp 'my IsEmpty';       r = 0;    end (3) At the prompt    >> r = testEmpty;
Workaround: 1. Rename the private function to a name different than the builtin (case insensitive)2. Use the exact case matched function name if the intention is to use the private function.

Summary: Incorrect digital I/O port updates when using unusual syntax for putvalue and getvalue
Description: When you use a line object twice, with putvalue on a NIDAQmx, you can get unexpected output values. getvalue also has similar issues. Example: dio = digitalio('nidaq','Dev1')addline(dio,0,0,'out')addline(dio,0,1,'out')putvalue(dio,[0 0])putvalue(dio.Line(2),1)  Both port 0, line 0 and port 1, line 0 will be set.
Workaround: Do not use line objects with putvalue and getvalue. Only use digitalio objects. For example, use putvalue(dio,[0 1]) instead of putvalue(dio.Line(2),1).

Summary: Constraining axes pan direction using setAxesPanMotion still enables the arrow keys to pan in all directions
Description:
You can call the setAxesPanMotion function to restrict the direction of panning to 'horizontal' or 'vertical' motion. A bug in this function allows you to pan the axes in an unrestricted manner using arrow keys. The following code demonstrates this issue: 
ax1 = subplot(2,2,1); plot(1:10); h = pan; ax2 = subplot(2,2,2); plot(rand(3)); setAllowAxesPan(h,ax2,false); ax3 = subplot(2,2,3); plot(peaks); setAxesPanMotion(h,ax3,'horizontal'); ax4 = subplot(2,2,4); contour(peaks); setAxesPanMotion(h,ax4,'vertical');pan on;
At this point, clicking within either of the bottom two axes and panning with the arrow keys results in an unrestricted pan.

Summary: Using a Scope (xPC) block in a model that is referenced in Accelerator mode causes Simulink to crash
Description: If a model is referenced in Accelerator mode and contains a Scope  (xPC)  block (which is from the library xpclib/Misc ) Simulink will crash if you simulate the parent model. The crash report will be similar to: ------------------------------------------------------------------------       Segmentation violation detected at Thu Apr 09 14:39:02 2009------------------------------------------------------------------------Configuration:  MATLAB Version:   7.1.0.246 (R14) Service Pack 3  MATLAB License:   unknown  Operating System: Microsoft Windows XP  Window System:    Version 5.1 (Build 2600: Service Pack 3)  Processor ID:     x86 Family 6 Model 15 Stepping 11, GenuineIntel  Virtual Machine:  Java 1.5.0 with Sun Microsystems Inc. Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM mixed mode  Default Charset:  windows-1252Register State:  EAX = 00002e00  EBX = 1501a1fd  ECX = 133177e0  EDX = 0000002e  ESI = 00000000  EDI = 15155ae0  EBP = 00cda6f0  ESP = 00cd9f14  EIP = 15012dd3  FLG = 00010246Stack Trace:  [0] scblock.mexw32:0x15012dd3(0x133178a0, 0x13315660, 0x133d04a0, 0x00cda720)This error was detected while a MEX-file was running.  If the MEX-fileis not an official MathWorks function, please examine its source codefor errors.  Please consult the External Interfaces Guide for informationon debugging MEX-files.If it is an official MathWorks function, pleasefollow these steps in reporting this problem to The MathWorks sothat we have the best chance of correcting it:  1. Send this crash report to segv@mathworks.com for automated analysis.     For your convenience, this information has been recorded in:       C:\Temp\matlab_crash_dump.5240  2. Also, if the problem is reproducible, send the crash report to     support@mathworks.com along with:       - A specific list of steps that will reproduce the problem       - Any M, MEX, MDL or other files required to reproduce the problem       - Any error messages displayed to the command window     A technical support engineer will contact you with further information.Thank you for your assistance.  Please save your workspace and restartMATLAB before continuing your work.-------------------------------------------------------------This segmentation violation occurred while executing the S-function 'referencedModel_msf' used by a Model block. Please report this problem to the MathWorks.
Workaround: You can work around this problem in either of two ways:Remove the Scope (xPC) block from the referenced model.Change the Simulation mode of the referenced model to Normal.

Summary: CellSelectionCallback executes when reordering columns in a uitable
Description: I have a uitable for which the RearrangeableColumn  property is set to 'on' . When I rearrange columns by dragging a column header, the CellSelectionCallback  executes, even if no table cells are selected.

Summary: collect does not group all the constant terms in an expression
Description: When executing the following code in MATLAB syms a b c d x;f = a + b*x + c*x^2 + d;g = f + a^2 + b^2*x;c1 = collect(f, x)c2 = collect(g, x) you get the output: c1 =c*x^2 + b*x + a + dc2 =a^2 + a + c*x^2 + (b^2 + b)*x + d collect  does not group all the constant terms due to reordering of the terms in sums done by the symbolic engine (MuPAD). The order of the terms in MuPAD outputs Resembles the common way of writing an expression Might differ from the internal order in which MuPAD stores the terms Might differ from the canonical form
Workaround: To prevent reordering of the terms in sums by symbolic engine (MuPAD), use the following command:evalin(symengine,'Pref::keepOrder(Always)');If you use this command, the symbolic engine returns results using the internal order. For example, entering the expressionb - ayou getans = - a + binstead ofans =b - a

Summary: Linearizing models with linearization I/Os on complex signals results in error
Description: When you use the linearize  command to linearize any Simulink? model with linearization I/Os on complex signals, you receive the following error:???? Error using ==> linearize at 188? The value of the "OutputName" property must be a string vector with as many entries as outputs. Type "ltiprops lti" from more information.
Workaround: Rename the file getioindices.m to getioindices_old.m. This file is located in the folder [matlabroot]\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@linearizationutil, where [matlabroot] is the MATLAB? installation directory you can find by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file getioindices.m and save it to the same directory.Restart MATLAB?.

Summary: MATLAB might encounter segmentation violation (crash) on Macintosh systems when concurrent license times out.
Description: When running R2009a on Mac OS X 10.5.6, and using a concurrent license with timeout enabled, MATLAB might encounter a segmentation violation (crash) when the idle timeout is reached.  Mac OS X 10.5.5 and other operating systems appear to be unaffected.

Summary: The cursor in an edit text box becomes offset from text that contains spaces in MATLAB 7.8 (R2009a) on Maci
Description: When there are spaces in text in an edit text box, an offset between the text and the cursor develops. The gap becomes larger as the number of spaces increases.
Workaround: To work around this issue, edit the java.opts file.For R2007b and earlier, you can find java.opts in Applications > MATLAB.For R2008a and later, it is in Applications > MATLAB > bin > maci (or maci64 for 64-bit platforms).Search the java.opts file for the following text:-Dapple.awt.graphics.UseQuartz=true Change the true to a false, to make it read:-Dapple.awt.graphics.UseQuartz=false Save the java.opts file. This change instructs Java not to use the Quartz version of Graphics2D when StartMATLAB launches.

Summary: Generated code for Prelookup block gives wrong numerical result when breakpoints have very large (multiword) power of 2, even spacing
Description: Real-Time Workshop generated code for the Prelookup block gives the wrong numerical result when all the following conditions apply: The breakpoint data are evenly spaced and non-tunable The spacing is power of 2: 2^m The input data type has scaling E_in, and the breakpoint data type has scaling E_bp, where m-min(E_in, E_bp) > hardware long  Suppose that: Input data type is sfix128_En96 Breakpoint data type is int8 Spacing is 2 and m = 1  m-min(E_in, E_bp) = 1 - min(-96, 0) = 97, which is bigger than hardware long 64. In this case, the generated code gives the wrong numerical fraction result.

Summary: Cannot cancel long searches in the Model Explorer
Description: For searches that take a long time, Model Explorer displays a dialog box containing a progress bar and a Cancel  button. However, the Cancel  button does not stop the search.

Summary: mmreader returns distorted images for selected video files on Windows? systems
Description: On Windows? systems, mmreader  returns garbled image data for selected files, including AVI files encoded in DivX? 5.0 formats. For an example of a distorted image, see the attached file garbled_image.png .
Workaround: For R2009a, download an updated version of the file directshowreader.dll:Find the $MATLABROOT directory where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB? root directory on your machine, as returned by typing:     matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file directshowreader.dll to directshowreader.dll.old.On 32-bit systems, this file is located in    $MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\bin\mmreader\plugins\win32On 64-bit systems, this file is located in     $MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\bin\mmreader\plugins\win64Download the attached file for your system:For 32-bit systems, download directshowreader32.zip.For 64-bit systems, download directshowreader64.zip.Unzip the file, and place the new file, directshowreader.dll, in the same directory mentioned in step 3.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Models containing Stateflow charts with multiple Simulink functions created in R2009a when saved to R2008b become corrupted
Description: To open a model with Simulink functions in Stateflow charts created in the R2009a version of MATLAB in the older R2008b version of MATLAB, you convert the model to the R2008b version by specifying the "Save as type" option as "Simulink 7.2/R2008b Models (*.mdl)" in the File > Save As  dialog box. Alternatively, you can perform this conversion using the MATLAB command:save_system('mymodel', 'mymodel_r2008b', 'SaveAsVersion', 'R2008b');  However, when a single chart contains multiple Simulink functions, this conversion leads to model corruption. Specifically, when you open such a saved file in the R2008b version of MATLAB, the Simulink functions seem to lose their contents.
Workaround: The workaround requires write access to the installation area of MATLAB R2009a. Follow these steps:Download the attached files saSFTargets.p and saSLFunctions.mStart MATLAB R2009aAt the MATLAB prompt, type:which @sl_saveas/saSFTargetsThis command provides a path to the location of this file in the installation area. For example, you see something like:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@sl_saveas\saSFTargets.p  % sl_saveas methodMake a backup of the file to which the above command points and overwrite it with the downloaded saSFTargets.p file.Name the backup copy saSFTargets.p.backup and move it to a location that is not on the MATLAB path.At the MATLAB prompt, type:which @sl_saveas/saSLFunctionsThis command points to a file path such as:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\toolbox\stateflow\stateflow\@sl_saveas\saSLFunctions.m  % sl_saveas methodMake a backup of the file to which the above command points and overwrite it with the downloaded saSLFunctions.m file.Name the backup copy saSLFunctions.m.backup and move it to a location that is not on the MATLAB path.NOTE: saSFTargets.p and saSLFunctions.m are supposed to be placed in different locations in the installation area for R2009a.  Also, storing the backup files in a location not on the MATLAB path prevents those incorrect copies from being used.

Summary: The second output of sort for sparse arrays input sorted along the second dimension is transposed.
Description: When sorting a sparse input along the second dimension, the second output is the transpose of the correct value. X = magic(3); [spY, spI] = sort(sparse(X), 2); [Y, I] = sort(X, 2); isequal(spI, I) % returns false isequal(spI.', I) % returns true
Workaround: When the first input to sort is sparse and the second input is 2, transpose the second output.X = magic(3); [spY, spI] = sort(sparse(X), 2); spI = spI.';

Summary: NEWPLOT and COLORBAR interaction causes SUBPLOT axes to shrink.
Description: Execute the following code: figure;subplot(221);newplot; colorbar;subplot(222);subplot(221);newplot; colorbar;  Now execute repeatedly: newplot; colorbar;  Each time we execute NEWPLOT  followed by COLORBAR , the left axes shrink.

Summary: Error thrown when indexing into large sparse matrices with linear indexing.
Description: If a sparse matrix A has dimensions such that prod(size(A)) is greater than 2^32 on 32-bit platforms or 2^64 on 64-bit platforms, then linear indexing into A may throw an error. An error will not be thrown if the index is a double scalar. For example: % Create a 2^17 by 2^17 sparse matrix with a 1 in the (1,1) position.A = sparse(1, 1, 1, 2^17, 2^17);v = A(1); % Gives the correct result with no error.v = A([1 2]); % Throws an error.v = A(uint32(1)); % Throws an error.
Workaround: Use subscripted indexing instead of linear indexing.

Summary: Compiled Report Generator applications ignore custom stylesheets
Description: Compiled Report Generator applications, when executed, ignore custom stylesheets and display an error message similar to: Could not find stylesheet "customStylesheet"Using default stylesheet
Workaround: Use the main MATLAB file to copy the .rgs file (the custom stylesheet) to a separate directory and add that directory to the path. For example, add these two lines to the beginning of the main MATLAB file before compiling:copyfile('customStylesheet.rgs', tempdir);addpath(tempdir);

Summary: Using setparam method for an instance of the xpctarget.xpc type object to set the values of multiple parameters might cause unexpected results
Description: Using setparam  method for an instance of the xpctarget.xpc  type object to set the values of multiple parameters (vector) might cause unexpected results. For example: setparam(tg, [0 1],{[1 2 3], [4 5 6]});setparam(tg, [0 1],{[1 2 4], [4 5 6]});
Workaround: As a workaround:Close MATLAB.Download the attached file setparam.p and save it to:matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\@xpctarget\@xpcRestart MATLAB.

Summary: SPTool errors when importing a filter in ZPK representation.
Description: When importing a filter into SPTool with a ZPK representation, an error occurs if the zero component is a scalar.

Summary: You may get an assertion when generating code from a model containing multiple Embedded MATLAB function blocks that use structures.
Description: You may see an assertion when generating code using Real-Time Workshop from your Simulink model under the following conditions: Your model contains multiple Embedded MATLAB Function blocks. At least two of the Embedded MATLAB Function blocks use the same kind of structure internally (that is, they use fields with the same size, shape, and complexity of data). None of the structures is a registered bus type.   You are more likely to encounter this bug if the Embedded MATLAB Function blocks contain any constant structures or use arrays of structures.  One possible assertion from this error appears as:  CGIR assertion in .\classic\cg_scope.cpp(XXX): 'sym==scope->head()'
Workaround: Add a dummy field so that the structures in each block are distinct.

Summary: Simulating Stateflow chart in Rapid Accelerator or External mode crashes MATLAB
Description: Simulating a model with a Stateflow chart using Rapid Accelerator or External mode can drain system memory and cause a MATLAB session to crash.

Description: If you attempt to import a Physical Modeling XML file using the mech_import  command, and the file has very long body names, then the import fails. If you attempt to import a Physical Modeling XML file using the mech_import  command, and the file has body names with special characters " and ), then the import fails. Workaround: Use the following steps to resolve this problem in MATLAB releases R2009a and R2008b.Rename the files$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@Graph\readAndSetBlockPosition.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@Updater\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@Updater\getEdgeHalfStr.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@BodyBuildInterface\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@BuildInterface\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@GroundBuildInterface\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@JointBuildInterface\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@SubsystemBuildInterface\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@SubsystemUpdater\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@UserBlockBuildInterface\getLayoutString.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@UserBlockUpdater\UserBlockUpdater.ptoFILENAME_old.pRename to getLayoutString_old.p, for example. $MATLABROOT is your MATLAB root directory, as returned by the command matlabroot.Download the attached UpdatedPFiles.zip archive. Extract the archive files into the $MATLABROOT directory. The files will extract into the appropriate directories under $MATLABROOT.Enter the following commands at the MATLAB prompt:clear allclear classesrehash toolboxcache

Summary: WebFigure failing to render resulting in error
Description: In some circumstances, an error is thrown from WebFigures similar to: Index exceeds matrix dimensions.Error   This is caused by an internal rendering function failing to render the figure properly. In MATLAB Builder NE, this error logs if logging is turned on. Otherwise, it manifests with a running WebFigure (the toolbar is active and rotating, showing a cube) but with a static image being displayed with the text: Issue displaying image. Please refresh.

Summary: The Pipe Bend block with diameter of the pipe greater than 0.129 m produces incorrect results
Description: The Pipe Bend block contained an error. The maximum diameter of the pipe was limited to 0.129 m, but it was not indicated in the documentation, and entering a larger value was not prevented in the block. As a result, usage of pipe bends with diameters larger than 0.129 m produced an incorrect result caused by extrapolation.
Workaround: The Pipe Bend block is a structural model built of the Resistive Tube and the Local Resistance blocks, connected in series. Build your own model using these blocks. The key parameters of the model, such as the pipe length and the loss coefficient, should be computed manually.

Summary: Selecting PolySpace menu options in a Simulink model displays a 'GenerateCodeInfo' error message.
Description: When you select the PolySpace menu options in a Simulink model, the following error message appears: Error evaluating 'OpenFcn' callback of SubSystem block (mask) 'test/PolySpace Menu'.Error using ==> get_paramblock_diagram does not have a parameter named 'GenerateCodeInfo'.  This error occurs only when using PolySpace Model Link TL with Simulink release R2006b , R2007a  or R2007b .This happens because the model property 'GenerateCodeInfo' is available only in Simulink release R2008a and later.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:Close Simulink.Navigate to the folder: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\PolySpaceModelLinkCreate backup copies of the following three files:PolySpaceForEmbeddedCoder.mPolySpaceMenu.msl_customization.mDownload the attached file (generatecodeinfo.zip).Unzip the file contents into the folder %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\PolySpaceModelLink.Open Simulink, then try the PolySpace menu again.

Summary: Error occurs when using LCC compiler to build a C library.
Description: Choose LCC compiler by selecting LCC with mbuild -setup . Create a C shared library project in deploytool  or by using mcc  command with -d  option.  An error is generated during mcc  execution.
Workaround: Backup mex.pl (in matlab/bin directory) by renaming it.Download mex.pl file from the patch and copy it to your matlab/bin directory.Rebuild the project in deploytool or use mcc to rebuild the shared library.

Summary: Joints possibly simulating with incorrect axes when machine is simulated in two dimensions
Description: In certain situations, a Joint block can simulate with an axis of motion different from what you specify in the Joint's dialog. That is, the Joint's motion is not along or about the direction you enter in the Axis of Action  field in the dialog. When this bug occurs, the incorrect joint axis is evident throughout the entire simulation. The machine behaves as if the you entered a different Axis of Action  value in the Joint block's dialog. For this bug to occur, the following conditions are necessary: You set or leave the Machine Dimensionality  in one or more Machine Environment blocks as Auto-Detect  (the default) or 2-D Only . The machine being simulated actually moves in two dimensions only. That is, its bodies can only translate in a plane and only rotate about axes perpendicular to that plane.  Even if both conditions hold, this bug is still unlikely to occur. Other, additional conditions related to the particular directions of the axes of all the Joint blocks in the model must also be satisfied to trigger the error. These conditions are difficult to specify in general.
Workaround: Set the Machine Dimensionality to 3-D Only in the Machine Environment block dialog.
