Description: This exception could be encountered in commands like ginput, gtext, or zoom. The stack trace will look like: Stack Trace: [0] hg.dll:void __cdecl gs_data3matrix_to_pixel(double *,double *,double *,struct GObject_tag *,struct Real_point3 *,int)(0x52780020, 0x573d0020, 0, 0x1cf5a990) + 633 bytes [1] hg.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall HgCurveDescriptor::transformToPixels(struct Real_point3 *,int)(0, 0x00989680, 0, 0x1cf5ad20) + 45 bytes [2] libmwgui.dll:bool __cdecl gui_objects::isCurveInRectangle(class gui_objects::CurveDescriptor &,class gui_objects::Rectangle2i const &)(528, 0x00c2d0d0, 0x00c2d2c8, 525) + 729 bytes [3] hg.dll:bool __cdecl CurveInRect(struct GObject_tag *,struct mwrect *)(0x1cf5ad20, 0x00c2d128, 0x00c2d154 "ˇč??", 0x7883e09b) + 78 bytes [4] hg.dll:_GO_ObjectInRect(0x1cf5ad20, 0x00c2d128, 0x1ce86388, 0x022a94e8) + 22 bytes [5] hg.dll:struct GObject_tag * __cdecl pt_in_object(struct mwpoint *,struct GObject_tag *,class Vector *)(0x00c2d2c8, 0x1cf5ad20, 0, 0x1ce86388) + 699 bytes

Summary: Calculating the norm of a small vector in Embedded MATLAB functions may generate a constant divide by zero in generated C code.
Description: Calling norm(x)  in an Embedded MATLAB function with a small vector x  may generate a constant divide by zero ˇŞ / 0.0  ˇŞ in C code generated with Real-time Workshop. The division occurs in dead code that is not eliminated after loop unrolling. The problem occurs only if loop unrolling is enabled and the length of the input vector x  is less than or equal to the loop unrolling threshold.
Workaround: In the Simulink Editor, select Simulation > Configuration Parameters.In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select Optimization.Change the Loop unrolling threshold value to 1, or to any number smaller than the length of the vector passed to norm.Note that the division by zero occurs in dead code, so it may not be necessary in all cases to eliminate it.

Summary: FIT function fails to fit exponential function with certain data
Description: With certain data sets, a rounding problem causes the "fit" command to produce an error ("Matrix dimensions must agree") when you try to fit an exponential function. The following example illustrates this problem. x = [-10.987; 9.76; 3.26; 6.76; -5.89; 3.78; -0.571; 6.69; -2.04; 4.74;    -3.08; -3.78; -4.96; 0.989; 8.32; 5.42; -8.27; 6.95; -6.52; 11.488    ];y = [186; 58.2; 80.2; 60.8; 140; 74.1; 89.5; 61.1; 105; 65.4; 110;    119; 120; 96; 59.7; 68; 163; 57.1; 152; 45.5    ];cf = fit( x, y, 'exp1' )  Matrix dimensions must agree.Error in ==> fit at 374   errstr = handleerr(es.identifier, es.message, suppresserr);
Workaround: Save a backup copy of the following file:matlab\toolbox\curvefit\curvefit\@fittype\private\sethandles.mEdit the original file in the MATLAB editor and find the following line:x1 = (min(x):(max(x)-min(x))/(n-1):max(x))'; % n > 1, single data It is line 246 in Release 2009a, Version 2.0 of Curve Fitting Toolbox software. It will be a different line number in earlier releases.Change this line to:x1 = linspace( min( x ), max( x ), n )'; % n > 1, single data

Summary: A call to a local function named eps may be overridden by the built-in eps function
Description: If a function named eps  is defined in the same MATLAB file in which it is called, and the arguments to eps  are constants, the built-in version of eps  will be called instead. In the following example, the built-in eps  function is called instead of the MATLAB file eps  function:    function epsTest   disp(eps(2))   function y = eps(x)   y = x;
Workaround: Either put the eps function in a separate MATLAB file on the path, or use a variable rather than a constant as an argument.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop might stop execution while generating code for a model with a Concatenate or a Merge block inside of a Signal Viewing Subsystem.
Description: Real-Time Workshop might stop executing while generating code for a Simulink model if ALL of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Signal Viewing subsystem . (Use the command get_param('subsystemName', 'SimViewingDevice')  to determine if a subsystem is a Signal Viewing subsystem.) An Inport block residing in the Signal Viewing subsystem is connected directly to a Concatenate block or to a Merge block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Inport block and the input of the Concatenate or the Merge block . Also, in the Signal Conversion Function Block Parameters pane, select the Override optimizations and always copy signal option.

Summary: Documentation for generalized Pareto distribution describes relationship to Pareto distribution incorrectly.
Description: The reference pages for the gppdf , gpcdf , gpinv , gpstat , gprnd , gpfit , and gplike  functions describe the relationship between the generalized Pareto distribution and the Pareto distribution incorrectly.
Workaround: The correct relationship between the two distributions is that the generalized Pareto is equivalent to the Pareto when its shape parameter k > 0, and when the location parameter theta = sigma/k.There is another relationship between the two distributions, as described in some of those reference pages: when k > 0, the generalized Pareto distribution is equivalent to the Pareto distribution shifted to the origin, regardless of the values of the location parameter theta and the scale parameter sigma.

Summary: xlsfinfo and the Import Wizard error when reading Excel files with sheets of types other than 'Worksheet'
Description: When an Excel file contains a sheet that is not of the type 'Worksheet,' but, for example, of type 'Chart,' xlsfinfo  and the Import Wizard generate an error of the form: ??? In an assignment A(:) = B, the number of elements in A and Bmust be the same. Error in ==> xlsfinfo at 80 indexes(i) = (strfind(class(sheet), 'Worksheet') > 0); ? ?
Workaround: Replace the file matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\iofun\xlsfinfo.m as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:    matlabrootQuit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\iofun\xlsfinfo.mtomatlabroot\toolbox\matlab\iofun\xlsfinfo.m.oldDownload the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\iofun\Restart MATLABIssue the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:    rehash toolboxcache

Summary: simple can return wrong results for expressions containing E as a symbolic variable
Description: The code syms E I; simple(E^(I*pi)) returns ans = -1 instead of ans = E^(I*pi)
Workaround: Use variable names other than E.

Summary: The baseboard serial blocks from the RS232 Mainboard sublibrary allow you to select invalid baud rates
Description: The baseboard serial blocks from the RS232 Mainboard sublibrary allow you to select invalid baud rates. From the Baud rate  list, the following baud rates are invalid: 921600 460800 230400
Workaround: As a workaround, do not select baud rates faster than 115200.

Summary: The Real-Time Windows Target product does not support the Simscape and SimDriveline products
Description: Real-Time Windows Target models cannot contain Simscape or SimDriveline blocks.
Workaround: As a workaround, do not use Simscape or SimDriveline blocks in Real-Time Windows Target models. Doing so causes an error.

Summary: Models that contain C64x DSP Library blocks fail to build for DM648, DM647, and DM643x processors.
Description: The DM648, DM647, and DM643x family of processors have C64x+ CPUs that are code-compatible with the C64x instruction set. Therefore, blocks from C64x DSP Library are compatible with these processors. Due to a bug, models that contain C64x DSP Library blocks fail to build for the DM648, DM647, and DM643x processors. The code generation process stops and generates the following error message: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'untitled'. MATLAB error message: The call to ccslink_ert_make_rtw_hook, during the exit hook generated the following error: An error occurred while validating the model during 'Exit' hookpoint. Caused by: The blocks from C64x DSP Library can be only used with the C64x family of processors. The build process will terminate as a result.
Workaround: To install the patch:Download the attached files, patch_installer.m and patch_533277.zip.Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt:patch_installer('patch_533277.zip', 'install')Complete the installation by following the instructions on the MATLAB command line.The attached patch files modify the following files in your MATLAB installation:matlab\toolbox\rtw\targets\ccslink\ccslink\c6000_validateModelExit.m

Summary: Minimum sample rate warning when adding channel to National Instruments USB-9234, WLS-9234, or ENET-9234 devices
Description: When you create an analog input object for the National Instruments USB-9234, WLS-9234 or ENET-9234, and then add a channel you receive the following warning message: ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');addchannel(ai, 2);  Warning: This hardware could not support the requested value of 1652.00 for SampleRate. SampleRate has beenset to 1706.67  This warning occurs due to a disparity in the minimum sample rate settings in the National Instruments devices. The device specification states that the minimum rate is 1652 and this is the value specified in the mwnidaqmx.ini file. However, when you calculate the sample rate using the formula specified in the NI USB-9234 User and Specifications Guide (http://www.ni.com/pdf/manuals/372307a.pdf) the actual sample rate is 1651.6129, not 1652. Here is the formula: fs = (fm / 256) / n  Where fm is frequency of the master timebase which is 13.1072 MHz; and n is any integer from 1 to 31. So the minimum frequency is 13.1072/256/31 = 1651.6129.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do the following:1. Find and note the location of the following file:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.ini(where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root installation directory obtained by issuing:matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)2. Quit MATLAB.3. Make a copy of this file and rename it to mwnidaqmx.ini.old.4. Open the file mwnidaqmx.ini in a text editor. Find the section for the device that you want to use searching for the name of the device:USB-9234WLS-9234ENET-9234Change the line:minAISampleRate = 1652tominAISampleRate = 1651.61295. Save the file mwnidaqmx.ini and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox clustergram function errors when data along the standardized dimension have a standard deviation of zero.
Description: Bioinformatics Toolbox clustergram  function errors when data along the standardized dimension have a zero standard deviation. This is caused by the introduction of NaN values when scaling data with a standard deviation of zero.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\@clustergram\mastandardize.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\@clustergram\mastandardize.m.old,where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\@clustergram.Restart MATLAB.Run the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Error Cannot find CTF archive when executing standalone with separate CTF file from another folder
Description: I compile the standalone as follows: mcc -m -C test.m   If I put the standalone application in a folder such as C:\Temp , and then execute it from the Windows command prompt as follows: > C:\Temp\test.exe   The first time I get the error (note the extension .ctf.ctf ): Extracting CTF archive. This may take a few seconds, depending on the size of your application. Please wait... The CTF archive 'C:\Temp\test.ctf.ctf' does not exist! Error initializing CTF Archive
Workaround: Ensure executable application file and CTF file reside in the same folder.

Summary: Invalid use of varargout may not result in an error.
Description: In some circumstances, it is possible for an invalid use of varargout  to be silently accepted and executed as if it were a valid statement. See the following example: function varargout = argOutTest  varargout = sin(3);   % Treated like varargout{:} = sin(3);>> argOutTest Note: If this code is run under the debugger, it generates an error.
Workaround: Fix the code to use the proper varargout syntax or disable the JIT using the command   feature accel off

Summary: TMTOOL cannot connect to USB devices
Description: TMTOOL will silently fail when you select a USB instrument node and press "Connect".

Summary: Help button for Parallel Options tab does not work
Description: Help button for the Parallel Options  tab is broken in the following: Options dialog box opened by clicking Estimation Options  in Control and Estimation Tools Manager when estimating model parameters Options dialog box opened by selecting Optimization > Parallel Options  in the Signal Constraint block when optimizing model parameters
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, please read the following:In the <matlabroot>/toolbox/shared/slcontrollib/@slcontrol/@OptionsDialog/configureButtons.m file, change helpview(mapfile, 'options_parallel') to helpview(mapfile, [prefix 'options_parallel']).Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: When a model that contains a Diamond Systems MM-32, MM-32X, or MM-32DX D/A block stops, the associated driver does not correctly reset the output
Description: When a model that contains a Diamond Systems MM-32, MM-32X, or MM-32DX D/A block stops, the associated driver does not correctly reset the initial output and reset output values. However, the run-time operation is correct.
Workaround: As a workaround:Close MATLAB.Download the attached file dadiamondmmx.c and save it to: matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Rule Viewer generates error when adding or removing input variables for Sugeno-type fuzzy inference system
Description: When you add or remove an input variable for a Sugeno-type fuzzy inference system in the FIS Editor, the following error is displayed at the MATLAB prompt when the Rule Viewer is open: ??? Error using ==> evalfismexInvalid FIS. PARAMS field of linear output MF has incorrect length.  Error in ==> evalfis at 84[output,IRR,ORR,ARR] = evalfismex(input, fis, numofpoints);  Error in ==> ruleview at 450  [v,irr,orr,arr]=evalfis(inputVector,fis, numPts);  Error in ==> updtfis at 44  eval([tag ' #update'])  Error in ==> fuzzy at 784  updtfis(figNumber,fis,[2 3 4 5 6]);??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback  This occurs because the FIS Editor fails to update the parameters of the output membership function correctly. ?
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, please read the following:1. Open the <matlabroot>/toolbox/fuzzy/fuzzy/addvar.m file and insert the following code before the line elseif strcmp(lower(varType),'output'):if strcmpi(out.type,'sugeno') && isfield(out,'output') && ~isempty(out.output)   for ct=1:length(out.output.mf)      if strcmpi(out.output.mf(ct).type,'linear')         out.output.mf(ct).params = [out.output.mf(ct).params -1];      end   endend2. Open the <matlabroot>/toolbox/fuzzy/fuzzy/rmvar.m file and insert the following code before the line case 'o':if strcmpi(fis.type,'sugeno') && isfield(fis,'output') && ~isempty(fis.output)   for ct=1:length(fis.output.mf)      if strcmpi(fis.output.mf(ct).type,'linear')         fis.output.mf(ct).params(varIndex) = [];      end   endend3. Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: Fit Options dialog fails to update when you change the polynomial degree
Description: When you change the degree of a polynomial fit in Surface Fitting Tool, the coefficients displayed in the Fit Options dialog do not update.
Workaround: To update the coefficients:Close the Fit Options dialog.Close the figure for the fit (do not delete the fit).In the table of fits, right-click and select 'Open "fitname"' to reopen the fit figure. You can also reopen the fit figure from the Fit menu.Click the Fit Options button to reopen the dialog with the correct coefficients.

Summary: Calling sum or prod on long vectors may give different results on each evaluation.
Description: Calling sum or prod on long vectors can produce a slightly different result on each evaluation. v = rand(1e5,1);sum_v = sum(v);sum_v - sum(v) may give a small nonzero result.
Workaround: (1) Disable multithreading in MATLAB? at startup.OR(2) Reshape the vector into a matrix and do an additional summation, e.g.sum(sum(reshape(v,n1,n2))) where length(v)=n1*n2, n1 < 80,000. For vectors where length(v) is prime, pad v with zeros for sum and ones for prod.

Summary: Incorrect code generation:  code execution result might differ from simulation for multirate multitasking model with Constant block as sample time source
Description: The result of executing generated code for a model might differ from the result of simulating the model if all of the following conditions are true: The model is multirate and multitasking. The model contains a Constant block with a nonconstant (non-inf) sample time. The Constant block is connected to a block with a faster rate, a continuous rate, or an asynchronous rate.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'C++ to intermediate language translation' with the message: '@FFT: call to an unknown procedure which was not properly stubbed: __nw__FUi'.
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C++ code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: **** Gnu C++ to intermediate language translation - 14 (P_VT) took 0real, 0u + 0s**** Gnu C++ to intermediate language translation - 15 (P_PT)----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: * fatal error, file: pst_file_main0.c, 40:2 :| call to an unknown procedure which was not properly stubbed: __nw__FUi|An internal consistency problem has been detected.|In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,|the computation has been halted.|Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support site|https://www.mathworks.com/support.|Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.----------------------------------------------------------------------...  This problem occurs when you are using the option -main-generator-calls  and  when the operators 'new ' and 'delete ' have been defined in the source code. For example, launching a C++ verification with the option '-main-generator-calls all ' on the following code causes this problem: #include <stdlib.h>void f() {} ;void *operator new(unsigned int sz) { return malloc(sz) ;}

Summary: When calling the BLSRHO function with no input arguments, it used to take vectors as inputs in the Financial Toolbox prior to R2008b.
Description: The BLSRHO function managed to take vectors as inputs in the Financial Toolbox prior to R2008b. But in R2008b and R2009a it is not possible to use a vector as input, when calling the function with no input arguments.
Workaround: Attached is the fix for the problem.To workaround this issue, replace the version of blsrho .m function in your current installation with the updated version attached.Exit MATLAB?.Make a backup copy of the following file.matlabroot\toolbox\finance\finance\blsrho .mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, blsrho.m.OLD. Download the attached file and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: "Report only if system has at least N subsystems" property does not work properly in System Filter component
Description: Generating a report that has a System Filter component with the "Report only if system has at least N subsystems" property set filters all systems, regardless of the number of subsystems in each system.
Workaround: 1. Quit MATLAB.2. From the MATLAB installation folder, navigate to toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_sys_filter.3. Make a copy of isTrue.p and store it in a safe location.4. Replace the original copy of isTrue.p with the attached copy.5. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Chirp block parameters are not tunable in generated code
Description: When you execute code generated for any of the RTW targets (RSim, ERT etc.), the tunable parameters of the Chirp block in Signal Processing Sources library cannot be tuned.

Summary: imhist function returns incorrect values for bin locations when called on indexed image
Description: When called on an indexed image, the function imhist  returns and displays incorrect values for the bin locations. This is true whether you call the function imhist  with or without output arguments. For example: J = imread('peppers.png');[x,map] = rgb2ind(J,50,'nodither');imhist(x,map) % no output arguments  Note that colormap  ends at 49 but data spreads to 255. [count,x2] = imhist(x,map); % output arguments  Note that x2  has values that range from 0 to 255, when x2  should be 0:49.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the imhist function in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file: matlabroot/toolbox/images/images/imhist.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: imhist.m.OLDDownload the attached file, extract imhist.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Missing entries in 'Certification Credit for PolySpace Verifier for C/C++' table
Description: In the PolySpace Client/Server for C/C++ Tool Qualification Plan  document, two entries in the 'Certification Credit for PolySpace Verifier for C/C++' table and the corresponding explanations are missing. The missing information relates to seeking credit for DO-178B, Annex A, Table A-5: Objective (3) 'Source code is verifiable' Objective (4) 'Source Code Complies with Standards'.
Workaround: Replace qualkitdo_polyspace_tqp.rtf and qualkitdo_polyspace_tqp.pdf as follows:Determine your root directory.Quit the MATLAB and PolySpace products.Download the attached file.Unzip the qualkitdo_polyspace_tqp.zip file.Copy qualkitdo_polyspace_tqp.rtf and qualkitdo_polyspace_tqp.pdf into root_directory\toolbox\qualkits\do\polyspace\r2009a.Restart the MATLAB and PolySpace products.

Summary: Simulation errors can be caused by MDL files and MEX files of the same name
Description: Simulation errors can happen if you have an MDL file and a MEX file of the same name in the same directory. If you save the MDL file and then try to simulate, you see the following error because the MEX file of the same name is called instead of the model:"modelname" does not exist on the MATLAB path, or is not a valid Simulink model nameThis problem is caused by a failure to obey the precedence rules for the MATLAB path if there are MDL files and MEX files of the same name in the same directory. The rules for precedence between an MDL file and a MEX file of the same name in the same directory, e.g. X.mdl and X.mexw32, are as follows: If the MDL file is loaded into Simulink, it should take precedence: which('X') should return "X.mdl" Otherwise, the MEX file should take precedence: which('X') should return "X.mexw32"  However, if you call save_system on a loaded Simulink model this can cause it to lose precedence over the MEX file. Even though the MDL file remains loaded in Simulink, the MEX file is returned by "which". ?
Workaround: You can fix the problem by closing the Simulink model and reloading it again:>> close_system X>> load_system XAlternatively, avoid the problem altogether by making sure that you do not have MDL files and MEX files with the same name in the same directory.

Summary: R2009a does not support ModelSim 6.5. Patch included to allow use of R2009a and ModelSim 6.5.
Description: EDA Simulator Link MQ 2.6 (R2009a) officially supports ModelSim 6.4, 6.3, and 6.2. Often, additional versions (both older and more recent) of the HDL simulator will also work with any given product release. However, ModelSim version 6.5 does not work with R2009a. This patch enables customers to use ModelSim 6.5 with R2009a. Attempting to run a cosimulation with an unpatched R2009a will result in the following type of error: Error reported by S-function 'shdlcosim' in 'my_cosim/HDL Cosimulation/S-function': Thefollowing signal names do not exist in the loaded HDL model. /top/sig1, /top/sig2, /top/sig3. Check HDL simulator waveform window for full hierarchical names.  ? ?

includes the following
