Summary: Generated code for Lookup Table (n-D) block has compilation error when generating shared utility functions for model with aliased data type
Description: Generated code for the Lookup Table (n-D) block with aliased output data type has a compilation error when the utility function is generated in a shared location, for example, when the block is used with model reference.

Summary: Calling a standalone application that is on the path on Linux causes it to fail.
Description: Calling a standalone application that is on the path on Linux causes it to fail.
Workaround: This problem manifests only when embedded CTF archives are used.To workaround the problem, build your application without using embedded CTF archives (the default). If you are using deploytool to create the application:Click Settings > GeneralClear the Embed CTF archive into the Application checkbox.If you are directly invoking mcc from either a MATLAB command prompt or a shell command prompt, invoke mcc with the -C switch. For example,mcc -Cvnm hello.m?

Summary: Embedded MATLAB sometimes passes structures by value even though you use the x=foo(x) idiom correctly.
Description: In Embedded MATLAB, using the idiom x=foo(x)  usually optimizes generated C code by passing the top-level argument x  by reference without a copy whenever the argument is used as both an input and an output. However, when the top-level argument is a structure, Embedded MATLAB sometimes passes it by value even though you use the idiom correctly. For example: function x = subfcn(x)t = x.f(5);x.f(5) = 1;x.f(6) = t+1;  This function uses the x=foo(x)  idiom, so x  should be passed by reference in the generated code. However, in the generated code, the structure is passed by value: static mystruct m_subfcn(mystruct eml_x){  mystruct eml_b_x;  eml_b_x = eml_x;  eml_b_x.f[4] = 1.0;  eml_b_x.f[5] = eml_x.f[4] + 1.0;  return eml_b_x;}  This bug is caused by one of our other optimizations transforming the code in such a way that it no longer matches the x=foo(x)  idiom. The bug will not occur for all examples which use structures, but there is no simple rule to predict which examples will trigger the bug. Although the generated code is sub-optimal, it will always compute correct answers. This bug can occur in Simulink, Real Time Workshop, and EMLC.
Workaround: Add a redundant assignment at the beginning of the Embedded MATLAB function. For example:function x = subfcn(x)x.f(1) = x.f(1); % Redundant assignmentt = x.f(5);x.f(5) = 1;x.f(6) = t+1;In the generated code, the structure is now passed by reference:static void m_refp1_subfcn(mystruct *eml_x){ real_T eml_t; eml_t = eml_x->f[4]; eml_x->f[4] = 1.0; eml_x->f[5] = eml_t + 1.0;}

Summary: Restoring library links to Signal Builder blocks causes errors that prevent model saving
Description: When you create a library link to a Signal Builder block in a library and later you disable the library link and then restore the library link, Simulink generates an error when you attempt to save the model: An error or warning occurred during a callback while saving <modelname>. The previously saved version of this file (if any) has not been changed.
Workaround: You can work around this problem by avoiding the Restore library link menu option by manually editing your model and library to acheive the same result. If you want to update the library, you need to replace its existing block with a copy of the disabled link block, and you need to replace the disabled link block with a new linked block.

Summary: When verifying Ada code, PolySpace verification stops during the CDFA phase with the error message: "unexpected node"?
Description: Due to an internal problem, verifying code containing a specific Ada construct can lead to the following error message: - analyzing TEST1.GENERIC_TEST : #<procedure>- analyzing TEST1.TESTPROC : #<procedure>analyze_discrete_subtype_definition: INTERNAL ERROR unexpected node #3110:{comes_from_source = true, sloc = "test2.adb" line 102, column 45, parent = 3119, variant = Identifier{chars = "ii", entity = 1856, expr_info = {etype = 1856, flags = {assignment_ok=false,do_range_check=false, raises_constraint_error=false,is_static_expression=false}}}}ana : INTERNAL ERROR unexpected node #3119:{comes_from_source = true, sloc = "test2.adb" line 102, column 34, parent = 1869, variant = Slice{prefix = 1867, discrete_range = 3110, first_itype = 0, expr_info = {etype = 3112, flags = {assignment_ok=false,do_range_check=false, raises_constraint_error=false,is_static_expression=false}}}}ana : INTERNAL ERROR unexpected node #1869:{comes_from_source = false, sloc = "test2.adb" line 102, column 34, parent = 1870, variant = Type_Conversion{subtype_mark = 3120, expression = 3119, expr_info = {etype = 1498, flags = {assignment_ok=false,do_range_check=false, raises_constraint_error=false,is_static_expression=false}}}}exception Match(Match) raised.FATAL ERROR for test1.atz This problem is partially caused by the use of a modulo type, but the cause of the error is really code-specific. For this reason, there is no workaround. If you are experiencing this error, send your code to customer support. We will investigate the problem and provide a workaround, if possible.

Summary: "Show revisions since date" option in Model Change Log component has no effect
Description: Changing the 'Show revisions since date' option in the Model Change Log component has no effect on the output of history change logs. The report displays all logs, regardless of the date filter setting.
Workaround: Install the attached patch:1. Quit MATLAB.2. From the MATLAB installation folder, navigate to toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_mdl_changelog.3. Make a copy of parseHistory.p and store in a safe location.4. Replace the original copy of parseHistory.p with the attached version.5. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: On Windows (XP or Vista) error R6034 is thrown by C runtime.
Description: On Windows (XP or Vista) error R6034 is thrown by the C runtime. This happens when executing standalone applications, C or C++ user applications built using C or C++ shared libraries, MATLAB Builder JA components, and MATAB Builder NE components. In this case, the user has a PATH directory containing msvcr80.dll . The error is thrown because the version of tbbmalloc.dll  in mcrinstallroot |matlabroot \bin\arch  does not contain a manifest. ?
Workaround: For standalone executables and C/C++ shared libraries using a supported Microsoft Visual Compiler:Download the zip files in the attachment. New option files have been provided for both 32-bit and 64-bit Windows. These new options files create executables with manifests which the operating system uses to locate runtime shared libraries in the Windows side X side area. Applications created using the attached options files can be distributed to deployed machines without any change to the target MCR install area.Back up matlabroot\bin\arch\mbuildopts by executing the following at the system command prompt: cd matlabroot\bin\arch & mkdir mbuildopts.bak & cd mbuildopts & copy *.*..\mbuildopts.bak where matlabroot is the MATLAB installation directory which can be found by typing: matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt and arch is the platform.Unzip either options_win32.zip or options_win64.zip to the root of your MATLAB installation directory.Execute mbuild -setup.Rebuild your application using the downloaded options files which you enabled by executing mbuild -setup.For standalone executables and C/C++ shared libraries Using Lcc-win32 compiler:Create a manifest file named application_name.exe.manifest (if creating an executable) or application_name.dll.manifest (if creating a shared library) with the following content:    <?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8' standalone='yes'?> <assembly xmlns='urn:schemas-microsoft-com:asm.v1' manifestVersion='1.0'>   <dependency>     <dependentAssembly>       <assemblyIdentity type='win32' name='Microsoft.VC80.CRT' version='8.0.50727.762' processorArchitecture='x86' publicKeyToken='1fc8b3b9a1e18e3b' />     </dependentAssembly>   </dependency> </assembly>You can create this manifest file by copying the above content into a text file.Place the manifest in the same folder as the executable. For example, if you have an application called matrixdriver.exe, you would create a manifest file called matrixdriver.exe.manifest and put it in the same folder as the application.For MATLAB Builder for JA and MATLAB Builder for NE, perform the following on your development machine:Change your folder to matlabroot\bin\archBackup tbbmalloc.dll and rename it to tbbmalloc.dll.bakExecute the following command at a Visual Studio Command Prompt: mt.exe -inputresource:libut.dll;#2 -outputresource:tbbmalloc.dll;#2tbbmalloc.dll now contains a manifest, and must be distributed to any machine on which you have encountered error R6034 when executing a MATLAB Builder JA or MATLAB Builder NE component. You should place the modified tbbmalloc.dll in mcrroot\bin\arch where mcrroot is the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR) installation directory.

Summary: Rapid Accelerator or Real-Time Workshop may assert when used with Embedded MATLAB library blocks
Description: When you use Rapid Accelerator or Real-Time Workshop with models that contain multiple Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in a library, you may get an assertion that looks like this: CGIR assertion in .\general\eml_tag_handler.cpp(158): '!fApiStack.isEmpty()' [0] libmwsimulink.dll:0x6f5c... [1] cg_ir.dll:0x0... The problem occurs only in the R2009a release.
Workaround: The attached version of sfc.m fixes the problem. Substitute sfc.m in your installation as follows:Use the ver command to confirm that your installed version is R2009a.Back up the installed version of sfc.m, which is located in the folder specified by fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox/stateflow/coder').Replace the installed version with the version in the attached zip file.Run the command rehash toolboxreset.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Accelerator mode simulation results using variable-step solver if model invokes reusable subsystem at different rates
Description: Simulating a model in Accelerator mode using a variable-step solver might generate incorrect results if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains multiple invocations of a reusable subsystem block. The reusable subsystem has one rate and is sample time independent. The blocks that invoke the reusable subsystem execute at different rates.  The cause of the error is that the software incorrectly generates an ssIsSampleHit  function call into the single-rate reusable subsystem code.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2008a, R2008b, or R2009a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/rtmdllib.tlcmatlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlcDownload the attached files rtmdllib_suffix.tlc and commonbodlib_suffix.tlc, where suffix corresponds to the last two characters of your MATLAB release number, and place the files at the same location as the files you renamed. Rename the files to remove the suffix. For example, for R2008a, download the files rtmdllib_8a.tlc and commonbodlib_8a.tlc and rename them to rtmdllib.tlc and commonbodlib.tlc.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: ?Class instance creation error and potential crash when working with .NET classes which have hidden (overriden) properties.
Description: The following example demonstrates a class instance creation error. Given the following C# class definition: namespace PropTest{ public class Base { public DateTime MyProperty { get { return dt; } } protected DateTime dt; } public class Derived : Base { public new string MyProperty { get { return dt.ToShortDateString(); } } }} Attempting to create an instance of the PropTest.Derived  class: obj = PropTest.Derived(); results in the error: ??? Error using ==> PropTest.Derived'MyProperty' is already defined as a property. If a hidden (overriden) base class property is abstract, attempting to call the property with the same name defined in the derived class instance causes MATLAB to crash. The following example demonstrates the crash: NET.addAssembly('System.Data'); dbProviderFactory = System.Data.Common.DbProviderFactories.GetFactory('System.Data.SqlClient'); dbCommand = dbProviderFactory.CreateCommand(); dbCommand.CommandText = 'command text';

Summary: Printing SimBiology diagram fails on Mac OS X.
Description: Printing a SimBiology diagram on Mac OS X platform is not supported.
Workaround: To work around this issue export the diagram as a .pdf or .svg file and print the exported file.In the SimBiology desktop, select Diagram > Export Diagram As.... The Export Diagram As dialog box opens.From the File type list select svg or pdf, and enter an appropriate file location and name.Click OK.Open the exported file and print it.

Summary: Timeseries plots can have unreadable or inappropriate XTicks in axes without normalized units
Description: The timeseries object plot method assumes that the axes has normalized Units when plotting data from a timeseries with an absolute time vector. When  this method draws into an axes with non-normalized units, the automatically calculated xtick positions can be unreadable or unsuitable.
Workaround: The easiest workaround is to set the axes units to normalized before plotting:set(axes_handle,'Units','normalized')?

Summary: copularnd sometimes generates an error for t copula parameters estimated using copulafit.
Description: For certain data, copulafit  may return an estimate of the t copula correlation matrix, some of whose diagonal elements are not precisely 1, but are instead 1-eps . If you use such a matrix as an input to copularnd , you get an error. For example: [Rho,nu] = copulafit('t',U);V = copularnd('t',Rho,nu,n);??? Error using ==> copularnd at 104RHO must be a correlation matrix.
Workaround: Replace the diagonal elements of the estimated correlation matrix with ones. For example:[Rho,nu] = copulafit('t',U);d = size(Rho,1);Rho(1:(d+1):d*d) = 1;V = copularnd('t',Rho,nu,n);

Summary: Library Info component does not appropriately sort by block
Description: Choosing to sort by block in the Library Info component does not function as expected. The generated report may show duplicate, missing, or unsorted blocks.
Workaround: Apply attached patch:1. Quit MATLAB.2. From MATLAB installation folder, navigate to toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@CLibinfo.3. Make a copy of execute.p and store it in a safe location.4. Replace the original copy of execute.p with the attached copy.5. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error or crash when setting EdgeColor to 'flat' or 'interp' on polygon constructed with geoshow or mapshow
Description: Setting 'EdgeColor' to 'flat' or 'interp' on a polygon constructed with geoshow or mapshow results in an error in R2009a. The command 'shading flat' will produce the same error (once per polygon, so there many be many repetitions). In addition, you may receive one or more warnings at the command line. In R2008a and R2008b, the same steps will cause a MATLAB crash. In R2006a, R2006b, R2007a and R2007b, they result in an error and a prompt to restart MATLAB. And in R14SP1, R14SP2, and R14SP3, they cause MATLAB to become unresponsive.
Workaround: Two separate workarounds are provided here. Use the first workaround to patch R2009a and use the second to patch R2008b and earlier. Each workaround requires that a single file be replaced, but the names and locations of the files are different in each case. This difference results from an architectural improvement introduced in R2009a.To work around this issue in R2009a, replace the mappolygon.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/private/mappolygon.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: mappolygon.m.oldCopy the version of mappolygon.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.To work around this issue in R2006a through R2008b, replace the Polygon.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/@MapGraphics/@Polygon/Polygon.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: Polygon.m.oldCopy the version of Polygon.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Selecting a valid database file for the J1939 Database (CANdb) Setup block might display No Database! in the block mask
Description: Selecting a valid database file for the J1939 Database (CANdb) Setup block might still display No Database!  in the block mask. As a result, you will not be able to select a valid PGN in the block parameter dialog box for the J1939 Transmit Message and J1939 Receive Message blocks. MATLAB might display the following error if you try to choose a PGN value: ??? Undefined function or method 'hexstr' for input arguments of type 'double'.
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached files j1939dbcload.m and j1939blkgui.m and save them to: matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\Restart MATLAB.

Summary: MATLAB terminates execution during the debugging of a script from the mask dialog parameter callback
Description: MATLAB terminates execution if the following conditions are met: You set the MATLAB Debugger breakpoint in the callback MATLAB stops at that breakpoint You call dbquit command or "Exit Debug Mode" from the Debugger menu.

Summary: If you use inline parameters with a model that contains baseboard serial blocks, the blocks do not work
Description: If you use inline parameters with a model that contains baseboard serial blocks, the blocks do not work.
Workaround: As a workaround, do not use inline parameters with xPC Target models that contain baseboard serial blocks.

Summary: Publishing to Microsoft? Word format: sometimes output is missing or incorrectly ordered
Description: If a MATLAB file contains nested cells, sometimes the output is missing or incorrectly ordered when you publish to Microsoft? Word.
Workaround: Follow these steps:Specify the publish output format as HTML. For example:publish myfile htmlOpen the HTML document in Microsoft? Word.Save the file as a Word document.

Summary: The "Debugging a Target Application" demo fails when using CCS 3.3 SR10.
Description: The "Debugging a Target Application" demo fails when using CCS 3.3 SR10. In CCS 3.3 SR10, breakpoints cannot be inserted because of the optimization (-o2) used in the demo project.
Workaround: Open the demo project in CCS and remove the optimization setting -o2:Open the project's Build Options dialog.Go to the Compiler tab and select Basic under Category.Change the Opt Level value from Function(-o2) to None.Rebuild the project.Rerun the demo.

Summary: The Sample time parameter in an Inport or Outport block dialog can be incorrectly disabled
Description: When the Specify properties via bus object  parameter is selected in an Inport or Outport block dialog, the Sample time  parameter is incorrectly disabled. Clearing Specify properties via bus object  re-enables Sample time . Even though Sample time  is incorrectly disabled, Simulink uses the displayed value of the parameter during simulation. The results of simulation are unaffected by the incorrect disablement.

Summary: MATLAB SEGVs on Debian 5 "Lenny".
Description: When starting MATLAB on Debian 5 "Lenny", it throws a segmentation violation on startup.
Workaround: The problem is that the version of xinerama in Debian 5 is inactive when there is only one monitor.Work around this problem by copying /usr/lib/libXinerama.so from a Debian 4.0 machine, and place it in sys/os/$ARCH. This will fix the MATLAB issue.To fix the next problem of a Java assertion/stacktrace, upgrade the JRE to 1.6.0 update 6 or later. (We tested update 12).

Summary: "Show all tabs" option in Dialog Snapshot only shows first tab
Description: When using Simulink Report Generator, if the "Show all tabs" box in the Simulink Dialog Snapshot component is checked, separate snapshots should appear for every tab on the dialog. However, only the first tab is actually being displayed.
Workaround: Install the attached patch as follows:1. Quit Matlab2. Backup the following file in a safe location: toolbox\shared\dastudio\@RptgenDA\@RptDialogSnapshot\traverseTabs.p3. Replace the original copy of traverseTabs.p with the attached file4. Restart Matlab

Summary: One-input form of max or min with sparse logical input may cause a segmentation violation.
Description: Using the one-input form of max  or min  with a sparse logical input may cause a segmentation violation in MATLAB?. The two-input forms of max  and min  work as expected.
Workaround: There are two workarounds. For input arrays that are sparse logicals:1. Cast the input to double before calling max or min.2. Use the any function in place of max and the all function in place of min.

Summary: Error during coverage reporting when more than 10 figure windows are open
Description: During signal range coverage reporting, an error might occur when more than 10 figure plots are present at the same time at the end of the simulation. If this happens, you see the following error message: %% --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------??? Error using ==> ReportScript.dump_model_signal_range>create_scriptStruct at 422 Invalid Simulink object handle.Error in ==> ReportScript.dump_model_signal_range at 23    this = create_scriptStruct(this);Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\toolbox\slvnv\simcoverage\cvhtml.p>dump_html at 384        cvi.ReportScript.dump_model_signal_range(outFile, topCvId, allTests, waitbarH, options);Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\toolbox\slvnv\simcoverage\cvhtml.p>cvhtml at 160        [sections htmlData] = dump_html(baseFileName, modelName, metricNames, allTests, this.waitbarH, options); %%
Workaround: There is no workaround for this problem.You can avoid the problem by using fewer figure windows or by setting the figure property "IntegerHandle" to "off" when they are created.

Summary: Using file names with certain characters for CANdb database files generates errors
Description: If you have CANdb files with certain characters or spaces in them, using these file names with Vehicle Network Toolbox MATLAB functions and Simulink blocks generates errors. In some cases, files appear to be compatible when you enter it, but when you subsequently load these files, they will cause errors. The following CANdb file names are incompatible with Vehicle Network Toolbox: Files containing spaces Files starting with numeric characters or underscores Files containing non-alphanumeric characters such an equal sign, ampersands, and so forth.  ?
Workaround: To work around this issue:Rename your files to contain only alpha-numeric characters and underscores.Use only alphabets at the beginning of the filename. File names cannot begin with numbers or underscores.Remove all special characters from your file names.?

Summary: Clicking links to subsytems in profiling reports produces errors
Description: This bug occurs when you: Configure a model with atomic subsystems to profile by "Atomic subsystems." Build the model from Simulink. Generate a profile report using "profile" command. Click a hyperlink with the name of a subsystem.  The error message is: ??? Error using ==> load_system at 29 There is no system named '<Name of the sub system>' to open.  ?
Workaround: Download the attached patch_installer.m and patch_529704.zip.Start MATLAB? and set the Current directory to the location of the downloaded files.Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_529704.zip', 'install')To complete the installation, follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line.The patch modifies the following files in your MATLAB installation:

Summary: SimMechanics visualization fails to display Body coordinate system and center of gravity if visualization contains only Body coordinate system and center of gravity
Description: If a Body block in a SimMechanics model: Does not have enough Body coordinate system (CS) origins to define a convex hull for the body, and Has its body geometry visualization option set to Convex hull from Body CS locations ,  then the visualization window might not show the center of gravity (CG) and CSs of the body when you select Fit to View  on the window. This failure can occur when a body has fewer than two noncoincident Body CSs, and a convex hull for the body cannot be defined. CGs are not included in the convex hull definition in this visualization mode. When you select Fit to View , the CSs and CG are not included in the objects required to be displayed, and Body CSs and CGs might not be visible at all. The problem is more obvious when there is only one Body in the model, because the visualization window fails to show anything when you select Fit to View .
Workaround: To resolve this issue, do one of the following:Add more Body coordinate systems to the body.Change the visualization of the body geometry to Equivalent ellipsoid from mass properties.Afterward, update the model diagram and perform a fit-to-view to see the changes in visualization window.

Summary: Bad topology possible in the polygon generated by framem
Description: For certain geometries, it's possible for function framem  to end up with a two-part polygon when constructing the frame patch. The display will look fine, but an error may result downstream in functions (such as tightmap ) that expect the XData and YData of the patch to define a single loop. The same problem can occur if the map frame is constructed indirectly, through a call to axesm , usamap , or worldmap , for example.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the framem.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/framem.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: framem.m.oldCopy the version of framem.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: PolySpace raises exception an exception Common_unimplemented(Common_unimplemented) when a type is derived, tagged and private.
Description: Problem: When verifying a code, PolySpace raises this error message : %%%MESSAGE%%%**** Phase 3 (Subprograms access processing - 1/2) took 0.2real, 0.1u + 0s **** Phase 4 (Subprograms access processing - 2/2) **** Phase 4 (Subprograms access processing - 2/2) took 0real, 0u + 0s **** Phase 5 (Tagged Objects Processing) Loading and analyzing ALL/_pst_mainUnloading ALL/_pst_main Loading and analyzing ALL/baseProcess_Tagged_Objects.Process_Tagged_Objects.process_derived_type_definition : error Node_Variant1917 : {comes_from_source = true, sloc = "my_package.ads" line 56, column 40, parent = 1921,variant = Derived_Type_Definition{subtype_indication = 1920, record_extension_part = 0}}exception Common_unimplemented(Common_unimplemented) raised.raised at: me95.sml:38829.45 me95.sml:39428.38 me95.sml:40240.78 me95.sml:31931.57 %%%MESSAGE%%% The error occurs with a code that contains a type that is derived, tagged and private.
Workaround: Since the error occurs with types that are derived, tagged and private, the workaround is to remove the type if possible, or remove the derivation of the type:Example:If the problem occurs withe the type:Type One_type is new One_Package.Sequence; replace it bysubtype One_type is One_Package.Sequence;

Summary: The struct passed to loadobj when an error occurs during load might not reflect the state of the object that was saved.
Description: When loading a saved object whose class has had a property removed, that property is not included as a field in the struct  that is passed as an argument to loadobj . In addition, fields in the struct  will have default values instead of the saved values, if default values were specified in the class definition. Consider the following class: classdef Class1    properties        ExisitngProp = 1        NewProp1    end    methods(Static)        function h = loadobj(s)            if isstruct(s)                h = Class1;                h.NewProp1 = s.OldProp1;                h.ExistingProp = s.ExistingProp;            else                h = s;            end        end    endend The field OldProp1  was not included in the struct  s  passed to loadobj . The field ExistingProp  had the value 1 instead of the value of the property as it was saved.

Summary: When checking MISRA coding rules, the analysis reports a false positive violation of MISRA rule 10.1 on unsigned bitfield for a 16 bit target.
Description: When using a 16-bit standard target like c-167 with the following code, the PolySpace MISRA-C checker incorrectly reports a MISRA 10.1 violation with the following message: ERROR : rule 10.1 (required) violated. At : misra.c:9 (column 26)| implicit conversion of the expression of underlying type 'unsigned int' to the| type 'unsigned short' that is not a wider integer type of the same signedness. typedef unsigned int int16_t; typedef struct T3ag{ int16_t Bit0_1:2; int16_t BiT1_3:2; int16_t Bit4_5:2; int16_t Bit6_7:2;} T3; typedef union T2ag{ T3 C2;} T2; typedef struct T1ag{ T2 C1;} T1; void main(void){ T1 V1; int tab[2][2];  V1.C1.C2.Bit0_1 = ((int16_t)tab[0][0]); // MISRA Violation}

Summary: If you access a private method of a class through a function handle, you might get an error like the following:Cannot access method 'myPrivateMethod' in class 'myClass'.
Description: This bug is caused by a MATLAB path change, either through rmpath  or cd , which temporarily removes the class from the path and invalidates the class's internal state.
Workaround: Make sure the relevant classes are on the MATLAB path.

Summary: Root Inport blocks with Bus objects specified can generate a misleading error message during compilation
Description: When the following conditions are true, Simulink will generate a spurious error message during model compilation: At least one root Inport has a bus object defined. The bus object has at least one element whose data type is another bus object. The number of bus elements in the root bus is different from the number of bus elements in the subordinate bus. The output of the root Inport block is connected to a Bus Selector or Bus Assignment block downstream.  The spurious error messaged appears whether or not any bus capable blocks exist between the root Inport and the Bus Selector. The message is similar to the following: When selecting signal 's1' from the bus created by block 'ExampleModel/In1', objects specified in the construction of that bus in turn specify that the bus object 'BusObject' is associated with the block 'ExampleModel/In1'. The bus object has 1 element, yet the block has 3 incoming signals.

Summary: CWT with complex-valued wavelet returns inconsistent GUI and command line results
Description: When applying the CWT with a complex-valued wavelet, the results returned in the GUI and at the command line do not match. The incorrect GUI result is the complex conjugate of the command line result.

Summary: Running a very large number of parfor loops or spmd blocks in a MATLAB session can result in the error message: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: GC overhead limit exceeded.
Description: If a single MATLAB client session runs many thousands of parfor  loops or spmd  blocks, it might eventually run out of memory, resulting in the message: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: GC overhead limit exceeded . The number of parfor  loops that can run before encountering an error depends on the size of the MATLAB pool. With a pool size of 30 labs, approximately 15,000 parfor  loops can execute before this error is encountered. The limit is not related to the size or type of the data sent to or from the parfor  loops or spmd  blocks.
Workaround: To work around this problem, you need to download and install the attached patch file appropriate for your system. The patch applies only to installations of R2009a. You should install the patch on your desktop machine running Parallel Computing Toolbox, and on all cluster machines running MATLAB Distributed Computing Server. To install the patch on a given machine, perform the following steps:Determine your MATLAB root directory for your desktop (client) machine by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Consult your system adminstrator to determine matlabroot for your cluster machines.Determine the MEX file extension for your desktop (client) machine and workers by executing the mexext command. For the desktop machine, type mexext at the MATLAB command prompt.For the workers, open a MATLAB pool from the client and execute spmd, mexext, endQuit the client MATLAB session and, if using the job manager, stop all workers.Renamematlabroot/toolbox/distcomp/distcomp/distcompMakeByteBufferHandle.mexext to matlabroot/toolbox/distcomp/distcomp/distcompMakeByteBufferHandle.mexext.old.Download the attached file appropriate for your machine type, and unzip it in the directory matlabroot/toolbox/distcomp/distcomp.Repeat steps 5 and 6 for all client and worker (MDCS) installations.Restart MATLAB and, if using the job manager, restart all workers.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate noncompilable code for models with a bus signal at an output port
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate noncompilable code if Generate reusable code  is selected in the Real-Time Workshop > Interface pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box and you are doing one of the following: Generating code for a model and the root output port is a bus signal Building a subsystem and the subsystem output port is a bus signal Generating a S-function target for a subsystem and the subsystem output port is a bus signal
Workaround: Designate the bus signal connected to the output port as an ExportedGlobal storage class.

Summary: Models containing the same name for Stateflow variables and Stateflow functions cause crashes.
Description: You might encounter this problem when running Simulink Design Verifier to analyze a model that contains Stateflow charts that have conflicting names for variable and function names. For example, you see this bug when the same name is used for a Stateflow function and a global data store variable.
Workaround: A manual workaround for this bug is to rename the conflicting variable or function names so that all names are unique.

Summary: Simulink does not detect multirate block in Action subsystem if block is not port-based
Description: Action subsystems do not allow multirate blocks inside of them. Simulink therefore reports an error if it detects a block with multiple sample times inside of an Action subsystem. However, Simulink fails to detect such blocks if they do not use port-based sample times. (Model blocks and S-Function blocks are the only candidates for being multirate blocks.)
Workaround: To detect multirate blocks, first turn on Sample Time Colors and look at the color of the Action subsystem. If the subsystem is yellow, then it is hybrid and might have multirate blocks. Therefore, look inside of the subsystem for yellow (multirate) blocks. Eliminate such blocks from the Action subsystem.

Summary: IRDataCurve and IRFunctionCurve seem to ignore Basis inputs
Description: When using the bootstrap  method of IRDataCurve , or the getZeroRates  or getForwardRates  methods of IRDataCurve  or IRFunctionCurve , in certain cases the Basis  input argument is not used to compute the output rates.
Workaround: This problem has been addressed in the attached files.

Summary: Simscape language components with discontinuities may generate more zero-crossing events in R2009a, compared to R2008b
Description: Simscape language components with discontinuities may generate more zero-crossing events in R2009a than in R2008b. The additional zero-crossing events may result in solver convergence issues or multiple zero-crossing failures. In addition, simulation performance may be negatively impacted.
Workaround: No workaround for this issue.

Summary: MLE function produces incorrect parameter estimates for a custom distribution when using a frequency vector that contains zeros.
Description: The MLE  function returns incorrect parameter estimates when fitting data with a custom distribution (i.e., when you use one of the 'pdf ', 'logpdf' , or 'nloglf'  parameters), if you pass in a frequency vector that includes any counts that are zero. MLE  produces correct parameter estimates in all other cases: When fitting one of the "built-in" distributions (i.e. when you use the 'distribution'  parameter), even with a frequency vector that contains counts that are zero. When fitting a custom distribution when there is no frequency vector, or when the frequency vector contains only positive counts.
Workaround: Before calling MLE, remove elements corresponding to zero frequencies from the data and frequency vectors, and from the censoring vector if there is one. For example, if your data, frequency, and censoring vectors are stored in the variables data, freq, and cens,if any(freq==0) data = data(freq>0); cens = cens(freq>0); freq = freq(freq>0);endif

Summary: You can only access 32 digital lines of the National Instruments PXI-6529
Description: You can only access 32 of the 48 digital input lines of the National Instruments PXI-6529 device.
Workaround: This occurs due to an error in the configuration information in the PXI-6529.To workaround this issue:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Find the following file: $MATLABROOT/toolbox/daq/daq/private/mwnidaqmx.ini (where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root installation directory obtained by issuing:matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)3. Make a copy of this file then rename that copy to mwnidaqmx.ini.old.4. Open mwnidaqmx.ini and find the entry for the PXI-65295. Change the lines for digitalPortSize and digitalPortCap to match the text below:[PXI-6529]hasAnalogInput = falsehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = truedigitalPortSize = 8,8,8,8,8,8digitalPortCap = 1,1,1,1,1,16. Save and close the file. 7. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Custom Simscape libraries fail to build when the sublibraries contain library icon files
Description: Custom Simscape libraries fail to build when the sublibraries contain icon files (lib.jpg, lib.bmp, lib.png) with the following error: Input argument "permuteImage" is undefined.
Workaround: To work around this issue:1. Download the attached file setImage.p and copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/ne_sli/@NetworkEngine/@Iconwhere $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.2. Download the attached file nesl_geticon.p and copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/ne_sli/private3. Restart MATLAB to make sure it is able to pick up the new files that you just downloaded.

Summary: Some complex functions in blas.h and lapack.h header files return incorrect data types.
Description: In the C header files blas.h and lapack.h in $matlabroot/extern/include, the return types are incorrect for functions that return FORTRAN complex types. Specifically, in blas.h, the affected functions are: cdotc cdotu zdotc zdotu  In lapack.h, the affected functions are: cladiv zladiv  In the header files, cdotc, cdotu, and cladiv are declared to return FLOAT, and zdotc, zdotu, and zladiv are declared to return DOUBLE. However, these functions actually return complex data types, which cannot be represented by FLOAT or DOUBLE.
Workaround: Edit the header files in the $matlabroot/extern/include. (You will need write permissions on the directory.) For cdotc, cdotu, and cladiv, change the function return types to a STRUCT that contains two member variables of type FLOAT. For example,typedef struct {float r,i;} complex;extern complex cdotc(  ptrdiff_t *n, float *cx, ptrdiff_t *incx, float *cy, ptrdiff_t *incy);Similarly, zdotc, zdotu, and zladiv can be changed to return a STRUCT that contains two member variables of type DOUBLE.

Summary: Translate block may error out when it is connected to the Merge block
Description: The Translate block errors out when the Interpolation method  is set to Nearest neighbor , and the output of the block is connected to the Merge block. The error message is as follows: "The signal from '.../Translate' output port 1 is required to be persistent, hence this signal cannot be connected to a Merge block."
Workaround: To workaround this issue, add a block to your model as follows:Place the Signal Conversion block from the Simulink? Signal Attributes library between the output of the Translate block and the input of the Merge block.

Summary: wavwrite creates improper WAV files with integer data
Description: wavwrite creates incorrect WAV files when the input array contains integer data. For example, the following code produces an invalid WAV file: SampFreq = 8000;ToneFreq = 660;BitDepth = 8;t=0:1/SampFreq:1;TestSound = uint8(sin(t*ToneFreq*2*pi)*100 + 128);wavwrite(TestSound,SampFreq,BitDepth,'WavTest.wav');
Workaround: Replace the file matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\wavwrite.m as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:    matlabrootQuit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\wavwrite.mtomatlabroot\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\wavwrite.m.oldDownload the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\Restart MATLABIssue the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:    rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Calling the xpcapi.dll xPCSetScope C API function from a stand-alone application causes a segmentation violation
Description: Calling the xpcapi.dll  xPCSetScope  C API function from a stand-alone application causes a segmentation violation.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following:Instead of calling the xPCSetScope function, call the corresponding scope set functions to accomplish the same behavior.According to your release, use the appropriate version of the attached xPCSetScope file, as follows:Download and unzip the attached file xpcapi_8b.zip or xpcapi_9a.zip, and save the unzipped file to: matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\api

Summary: PolySpace installation does not detect Telelogic Rhapsody? version 7.3 on a Windows PC.
Description: The PolySpace installer does not detect Telelogic Rhapsody? 7.3 on a Windows PC, therefore the PolySpace UML Link RH product is not installed. This occurs because the installer assumes that the 'rhapsody.ini ' file is located in 'C:\WINNT ' or 'C:\WINDOWS '. This is not the case in version 7.3 of Telelogic Rhapsody?.
Workaround: To workaround this problem:Copy the file 'rhapsody.ini' from the Rhapsody install folder to 'C:\WINNT' or 'C:\WINDOWS'.Re-install PolySpace Client For C/C++.Copy the file 'rhapsody.ini' from 'C:\WINNT' or 'C:\WINDOWS' back to the Rhapsody install folder.

Summary: Error initializing array, invalid dimensions message when trying to access a struct through MATLAB Builder EX
Description: I have a simple M-function that returns an empty structure, a 0-by-0 struct array with no fields. A simplified version of the M-function is: function out = emptyStruct  out = struct([]);  If this M-function is compiled into a MATLAB Builder EX component and called from Microsoft Excel, I get the following error message: Error initializing array, invalid dimensions  ? ? ?

Summary: Selection-based linking to Excel spreadsheets does not honor the option to omit path information.
Description: When you choose to reference external documents using no path information (because those documents are located on the MATLAB path), Excel documents continue to be referenced using their absolute path. ?
Workaround: You can work around this problem by modifying the file matlab/toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/private/selection_link_excel.m and changing the line of code:reqstruct.doc = docPath; to be:reqstruct.doc = storagePath;

Summary: MATLAB crashes when custom reference and measured disturbance signals are used with simulation start time specified as a workspace variable
Description: MATLAB crashes when simulating a model with MPC Controller or Multiple MPC Controllers block when both of the following conditions exist: The start time is specified as a workspace variable Reference signal  and Measured disturbance  options are selected in the block parameters dialog box  The crash is related to bad memory allocation because the start time becomes invalid under the above scenario.
Workaround: When custom signals are used in MPC blocks, do not set simulation start time as a workspace variable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation and code generation can result when Bus Selector block connects to Reshape, Signal Specification, or Bus Creator block
Description: Simulation and code generation for a model might be incorrect if a Bus Selector block connects to one of the following blocks: Reshape block Signal Specification block which changes signal dimensions Bus Creator block where the Bus Selector block output and element of the Bus object  of the Bus Creator block have a different number of dimensions, which are either N , [N 1] , or [1 N]
Workaround: Designate the output signal of the Bus Selector block as a test point.

Summary: An error occurs when you use putvalue on some National Instruments PXI-256x devices
Description: When you use putvalue  on a PXI-2564, PXI-2565, PXI-2567, PXI-2568 or PXI-2569, you will receive the following error: dio = digitalio('nidaq','PXI1Slot2')addline(dio,0:32,'out')val = dec2binvec(12, 32)putvalue(dio,val)??? NIDAQmx error : Measurements: Physical channel specified does not exist on thisdevice. Refer to the documentation for channels available on this device.Device: PXI1Slot2 Physical Channel Name: port2 Task Name: MWDAT0001 StatusCode: -200170
Workaround: Use this workaround if you are using MATLAB R2009a or a previous version.Change the configuration file for your device as described:Quit MATLAB.Find the following file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/daq/daq/private/mwnidaqmx.ini(where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root installation directory obtained by issuing:matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Make a copy of this file then rename that copy to mwnidaqmx.ini.old.Open mwnidaqmx.ini and find the entry for your specific device: PXI-2564, PXI-2565, PXI-2567, PXI-2568 or PXI-2569Change the lines for digitalPortSize and digitalPortCap to match the text below:[PXI-2564]hasAnalogInput = falsehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = truedigitalPortSize = 16digitalPortCap = 2[PXI-2565]hasAnalogInput = falsehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = truedigitalPortSize = 16digitalPortCap = 2[PXI-2566]hasAnalogInput = falsehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = truedigitalPortSize = 16digitalPortCap = 2[PXI-2567]hasAnalogInput = falsehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = truedigitalPortSize = 32digitalPortCap = 2[PXI-2568]hasAnalogInput = falsehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = truedigitalPortSize = 31digitalPortCap = 2[PXI-2569]hasAnalogInput = falsehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = truedigitalPortSize = 32digitalPortCap = 2Save and close the file.Restart MATLAB.NOTE: You can only access the first 32 digital lines on the PXI-2567 and PXI-2569.

Summary: Compiled model cannot be loaded if MATLAB search path is too long
Description: If the MATLAB search path has more than 32500 characters, Real-Time Windows Target kernel cannot locate and load the compiled model binary.
Workaround: As a workaround, ensure that the MATLAB search path contains less than 32500 characters.

Summary: The Image Acquisition Tool (IMAQTOOL) appears to hang upon start in Image Acquisition Toolbox?.
Description: When the Image Acquisition Tool (IMAQTOOL) is started, you may see the full desktop appear on your screen, but be unable to use it due to your mouse cursor showing the program is busy (usually this is indicated in Windows by an hourglass mouse pointer).
Workaround: 1. Download the file that matches your version of MATLAB.2. Unzip the initialize.p file contained within the file to:<MATLAB>\toolbox\imaq\imaq\@iatbrowser\@PreviewPanelController\initialize.p3. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Symbolic integrals can be slow
Description: In some cases, the computation of symbolic integrals takes a long time. When started in the MuPAD Notebook Interface, the following loop runs for over an hour:  for m from 0 to 10 do  for i from 0 to m do    for j from 0 to m - i do      p := i:      q := 2 * j:      t := 2 * m - 2 * i - 2 * j:      g := int(cos(2*x)^p * cos(x)^q * sin(x)^t, x=0..2*PI):      print(m,i,j,g)    end_for:  end_for:end_for:

Summary: PolySpace results display a false red IDP after a cast to void * and pointer arithmetic.
Description: PolySpace verification reports a false red IDP after a cast to void * and pointer arithmetic. The following example illustrates this behavior: void f(void) {  unsigned char c[9]; void *p; unsigned int *q; p = &c[1]; q = p; // implicit cast *q = 1; q++; *q = 2; // False RED}
Workaround: You can bypass the false red check by using the option '-size-in-bytes'.

Summary: Navigation to Excel fails from if the target cell range is not on the first worksheet.
Description: When you create a requirements link to an Excel spreadsheet location on a worksheet other than the first worksheet, the navigation fails, producing the error message "Location Not Found."

Summary: MATLAB may crash if a linked block having a load function is opened
Description: If the Model Browser is displayed, and a linked block that modifies its properties in its load function is opened, a segmentation violation occurs.
Workaround: Turn off the Model Browser for the model.

Summary: Using Simulink external mode to connect a target PC to a model that contains blocks with parameters of complex data types causes an error
Description: Using Simulink external mode to connect a target PC to a model that contains blocks with parameters of complex data types causes an error like the following: Executing 'SetParam' command produced the following error: Complex numbers not permitted as argument. The xPC Target product does not support the instrumentation of complex numbers (for example, tunning parameters of complex data types, monitoring signals, and tracing signals).
Workaround: As a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file capi_xpc.tlc and save it to: matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlcRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Some calls to solve involving 4th degree polynomials run slowly compared to MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a)
Description: Some systems involving fourth degree polynomials may cause solve  to run slowly. For example: syms x y g = (x^4 + 2*x^3*y - 6*x^2*y^2 + y^4)/(x^4 + y^4 + 1) gradg = jacobian(g,[x,y]) [xcr, ycr] = solve(gradg(1), gradg(2))

Summary: Potential incorrect setting of data type and output port complexity for a Reshape block with reference input port
Description: For the Reshape block with a reference input port, Simulink may not set the data type and complexity of the output port correctly in the following cases: If the data type of the data input port (port 1) is a subset of the data type of the reference input port, then the output port inherits the data type from the reference input port (port 2) instead of the data input port. For example, if the data input port has a 'single' data type and the reference input port has a 'double' data type, the output port will be set to 'double' data type. If the data input port is a real signal and the reference input port is a complex signal, the output port of the Reshape block is a complex signal instead of a real signal. For example, if the data input port complexity is 'real' and the reference input port is 'complex', then the output port is set to 'complex'.
Workaround: For a Reshape block with a reference input port, if the data type of the data input port is a subset of the data type of the reference input port, the workaround is to insert a Data Type Conversion block between the reference input port and the block that drives the input port.For a Reshape block with a reference input port, if the data input port is a real signal and the reference input port is a complex signal, the workaround is to insert a Complex to Real-Imag block (with the "Output" parameter set to "Real" or "Img") between the reference input port and the block that drives the input port.

Summary: jackknife function can return unexpected results for long vector-valued statistics.
Description: jackknife  accepts a user-supplied function to compute statistics on a data set, and applies that function to different subsets of the input data matrix. If the function returns a vector of statistics, and if that vector is longer than the number of rows in the input data matrix, the output from jackknife  may have extra rows.
Workaround: There should be one row in the output matrix for each row of the input matrix. Remove any excess rows.

Summary: Hydraulic Fluid block computes wrong values of kinematic viscosity for water and fuels
Description: If you are using Water , Water-Glycol 60/40 , Jet fuel , Diesel fuel , or Gasoline , the Hydraulic Fluid block uses incorrect formula to compute the values of fluid viscosity based on system temperature.
Workaround: To assure that the viscosity of water and fuels is computed correctly, follow these steps:1. Download the attached file sh_stockfluidproperies_priv.p.2. Copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/sh/sh/private/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.3. Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: HDL code generated for Stateflow arithmetic expressions on fixed point data types with scaling may produce incorrect results.
Description: HDL code generated from Stateflow arithmetic expressions may be erroneous under the following conditions: The operands have fixed point data types The operands have non-zero scaling The expression contains more than one operation One (or more) of the operations is a multiplication In one (or more) of the multiplication operation, the sum of the word-lengths of the multiplicands is greater than 32  In such cases, overflow or underflow may occur and the Simulink simulation results may not match the HDL Simulation results.
Workaround: Break up the expression into a series of expressions containing only 1 operator. For example, consider the expression y = a + b * c, where a, b, and c are of type sfix32en15. Since the previously listed conditions are met the generated HDL code may be incorrect. To fix the problem, you can rewrite this as two expressions, storing the multiplication result in a variable t, of type sfix32en15:t = b * c;y = a + t;

Summary: The location of text within a textbox annotation is incorrect when the VerticalAlignment property of the annotation is set to cap or baseline.
Description: This issue occurs when setting the VerticalAlignment  property of a textbox annotation to cap  or baseline . The following code demonstrates the issue: t = annotation('textbox','String','Hello World!','Position',[.2 .2 .2 .2],'VerticalAlignment','baseline');
Workaround: Set the VerticalAlignment property to top instead of cap, or to bottom instead of baseline.

Summary: A bug in array expansion using subscripted assignment of handle object arrays can result in arrays that contain repeated values of the same handle.
Description: There is a bug in subscripted indexing into MATLAB handle objects that affects code of the form: classdef Class_1 < handle  properties  prop = 0;  end  methods  function this = Class_1(n)  if nargin > 0  % create the array  this(n).prop = n;  end  end  end  end    >> c(1:10) = Class_1(10);  >> c(1) == c(10)    In the above case, c(2)  and c(10)  are erroneously assigned to the same handle. Each handle in array c  should be unique.

Summary: If initialization of the first Symbolic Math Toolbox command that calls MuPAD is terminated with CTRL-C, the next commands fail.
Description: If the first Symbolic Math Toolbox command that requires MuPAD is terminated with CTRL-C, MuPAD doesn't properly re-initialize when using next MuPAD commands. Next commands error with "out of memory" message: Out of memory. Type HELP MEMORY for your options.

Note:  This error can occur with PolySpace for C/C++, or PolySpace for Ada. ?

Summary: GRAPHALLSHORTESTPATHS function and ALLSHORTESTPATHS method for BIOGRAPH objects returns invalid, unreacheable nodes.
Description: GRAPHALLSHORTESTPATHS  function and ALLSHORTESTPATHS  method for BIOGRAPH  objects returns invalid, unreacheable nodes. Unreachable nodes are denoted with a distance of Inf  in the output distance matrix. There are cases in which nodes are reachable.
Workaround: Shut down all MATLAB sessions, or type "clear mex" at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached files graphalgs.{ARCH}, where {ARCH} is(are) the appropriate mex file suffix(es) for your machine architecture(s). For details about mex file extensions, see the MEXEXT function. Install the downloaded file(s) in the {MATLABROOT}/toolbox/bioinfo/graphtheory/private directory, where {MATLABROOT} is the directory where MATLAB is installed.After installing the file(s), force MATLAB to update the toolbox path cache file by doing either of the following:Select File > Preferences > General, then click the Update Toolbox Path Cache button in the Preferences dialog boxIssue the command "rehash toolboxcache" at the MATLAB command promptFor more information, type "help toolbox_path_cache".

Summary: Incorrect Answer: Wrong answer or spurious error in the presence of output events of either-edge type and Simulink functions in Stateflow charts
Description: When a Stateflow chart contains both embedded Simulink functions and output events of either-edge type, Stateflow produces incorrect results or throws a spurious error. When the data type of the first inport of the embedded Simulink function is marked as boolean  or Inherited , Stateflow produces incorrect results. For example, in the attached model, bug_wrong_answer.mdl, the expected value of Out1 is 2. However, Stateflow incorrectly produces a constant 0 for Out1. When the data type of the first inport of the embedded Simulink function is marked to anything other than boolean  or Inherited , Stateflow throws a spurious error about data type mismatch between the first inport of the Simulink function. The attached model, bug_error.mdl, shows this scenario. This model is the same as bug_wrong_answer.mdl except that the data type of the inport 'uu' inside the Simulink function has been explicitly set to double . In this case, Stateflow produces a spurious error.
Workaround: If possible, consider using output events of function-call type. Using that type of output event avoids the issue.If output events of either-edge type are needed to drive triggered subsystems outside Stateflow charts, consider using a function-call output event from the chart containing the embedded Simulink functions and then converting these events to either-edge output events as shown in the attached model, workaround.mdl.

Summary: nlmefit function ignores some 'ApproximationType' settings
Description: The nlmefit  function provides an 'ApproximationType'  argument to specify the type of likelihood approximation to use. If you specify the value 'FO'  or 'FOCE'  for this argument, the function ignores those settings and uses the 'LME'  approximation instead. If you specify the value 'LME'  or 'RELME' , the function correctly uses the approximation you specify.
Workaround: Follow these steps as a workaround for this problem:1) Quit MATLAB.2) Rename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\stats\nlmefit.m to nlmefit.old (where $MATLAB = the MATLAB root directory on your machine)3) Copy attached file to: nlmefit.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\stats\4) Restart MATLAB.5) After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: PolySpace verification stops with the message "@FFT: illegal call to <package2._PST95_L_procedure2>$2, #actuals=<nb_actuals>, #formals=<nb_formals>."?
Description: This error occurs only on a specific construct. The error message will appear as follows: - analyzing <package.procedure> : #<procedure>----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: illegal call to <package2._PST95_L_procedure2>$2, #actuals=<nb_actuals>, #formals=<nb_formals>An internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,the computation has been halted.Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support sitehttps://www.mathworks.com/support.Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.  The names <package.procedure>, <package2._PST95_L_procedure2>, <nb_actuals>, <nb_formals> will be different. For <package2._PST95_L_procedure2>, the keyword _PST95_L_ will still be there. The reason for the error is code-specific, so there is no easy workaround. If you experience this error, send the affected code to customer support. We will investigate the problem and provide a workaround, if possible. ?

Summary: Using absolute-time temporal logic operators in Stateflow charts sometimes leads to spurious error message about non-integer code
Description: Users of Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder code generation software have the option to lock down the fact that there are no floating-point variables, constants, or operations in the generated code. This check is performed if the user clears the floating-point numbers  check box on the Real-Time Workshop > Interface pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. This check box is visible only when ert.tlc  is selected as the System target file . When Stateflow charts containing absolute-time temporal logic are used in conjunction with this error checking, a spurious error about the use of non-integer code appears. This behavior does not apply when charts use event-based temporal logic.
Workaround: The workaround is to temporarily turn off the Stateflow check for non-integer code while generating Real-Time Workshop code.  Enter the following command on the MATLAB prompt:sf('feature', 'Non Integer Code Error', 'off')The above command has no effect on the generated code and does not imply that you will begin seeing floating-point constants, variables, or expressions if the code was previously integer only.

Summary: Simulink incorrectly reports an error for sample time specifications of [0,1] for specific blocks
Description: The blocks listed below require a discrete sample time in order to simulate properly. Digital Filter Digital Clock Discrete Integrator Discrete State Space Discrete Transfer Function Discrete Zero Pole Zero Order Hold  In releases prior to R2008b, these blocks accept a sample time of fixed in minor step, [0,1]. Simulink then converts the sample time to the model's fixed step size for models using a fixed-step solver. For models using a variable-step solver, specifying fixed in minor step results in an error. In R2008b and R2009a, Simulink reports an error even when the model uses a fixed-step solver.
Workaround: Specify the fixed step size in the Block Parameters dialog. Tip: You can use a MATLAB variable in both the Block Parameters dialog and the Configuration Parameters dialog to enable the changing of all instances simultaneously.

Summary: RF Tool does not import frequency range information from data files
Description: After importing a data file, the RF Tool dialog initially uses a fixed default frequency range for analysis and plot. This can produce unexpected results if the data file specifies a different frequency range. Rather than manually updating this information from within the dialog, you may prefer to have the tool import it from the file by default.
Workaround: For R2009a, replace the rftool.p file using the steps given below.Enter the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Navigate to the matlabroot/toolbox/rf/rftool directory, where matlabroot is the directory returned by the matlabroot command.Rename the file rftool.p as rftool.p.old.Download the attached rftool.p file to the current directory.Enter the command rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command prompt.For R2008b and earlier releases, contact MathWorks technical support.

Summary: If all objects in an array have a dynamic property with the same name, calling the set method of the hgsetget class on this object array to assign values to the dynamic property might not assign values to the property correctly.
Description: Given an object array, obj , in which the class of obj  is derived from both hgsetget  and dynamicprops , you can add dynamic properties to the individual objects of obj  using the addprop  method. If every object in the array has a dynamic property of the same name, then calling set  in the form:  set(obj,'DynamicPropName ',value)  sets the value on only the first object in the array. This problem occurs only if obj  is non-scalar.
Workaround: To work around this problem, loop over the individual elements of obj to call set on each object individually:for k=1:numel(obj)    set(obj(k), 'DynamicPropName', value); end

Description: During installation of MATLAB Builder NE and/or MATLAB Builder EX products on Windows or Windows x64, the user may see the following message appear in a pop up dialog: On Windows 32: - External process (C:\WINNT\system32\regsvr32.exe /s ˇ°C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\bin\win32\mwcomutil.dllˇ±) did not return after 10000 milliseconds   On Windows x64: - External process (C:\WINNT\system32\regsvr32.exe /s ˇ°C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\bin\win64\mwcomutil.dllˇ±) did not return after 10000 milliseconds

Summary: SOA jobs on Microsoft HPC Server 2008 are canceled if any task takes longer than 10 minutes
Description: MATLAB automatically cancels any SOA jobs that were submitted to a Microsoft HPC Server 2008 cluster if any task in that job takes longer than 10 minutes to execute. The job cancellation occurs 10 minutes after the offending task is started, and any pending tasks are not evaluated. This 10-minute period includes any MATLAB worker startup time and does not necessarily correspond to the evaluation time of the task.
Workaround: Download the attached MdcsService.dll.config file and copy it into the matlabroot\toolbox\distcomp\bin\win64 directory on all worker nodes.

Summary: Wrong Answer: Incorrect behavior of at() temporal logic operator under rare circumstances
Description: Under rare circumstances, the at() temporal logic operator in a Stateflow chart behaves wrongly like the after() temporal logic operator. In order for the wrong behavior to occur, the following conditions must hold: The first argument to the at() operator must have a value of the form (2^N) - 1, such as 3, 7, 255, etc. There are no other temporal logic operators associated with that state that count to a larger value.  Under the above conditions, the at() temporal logic operator wrongly behaves like the after() temporal logic operator. To see the wrong behavior, please see the attached model bug_at.mdl. The expected behavior of this model is that the counter y increments exactly once at time t = 7. The correct final value should therefore be y = 1. However, in R2009a or earlier, y keeps incrementing after t = 7. Therefore the incorrect final value is y = 4.
Workaround: In order to workaround this bug, you can create an empty conditional section, which uses the at temporal logic operator for the same event but with a larger count. See the attached model bug_at_workaround.mdl. In this model, the state has an empty conditional section on after(10, tick). This section gets eliminated from the generated code, so there is no code generation penalty for this workaround.

Summary: fread, fscanf, and sscanf do not allow size arguments that are non-double integers
Description: For example, let fid  be the output of an fopen  command. The following call to fread  generates an error:     >> n = int32(3);    >> c = fread(fid,n);    ??? Error using ==> fread    Size must be of type double.    >>
Workaround: Explicitly convert the size argument to double. For example:    >> n = int32(3);    >> c = fread(fid,double(n));    >>

Summary: PolySpace does not locate checks correctly when using the option -detect-unsigned-overflows
Description: When using -detect-unsigned-overflows , PolySpace verification puts OVFL checks in the wrong location. This problem occurs particularly with boolean operators. In the following example, checks concerning 'conversion from unsigned int32 to int 32 ' are located on the E  variable instead of B : unsigned char A, B, C, E;int foo(void){unsigned char D = 0;   A = B && C && D && E;return 0;}   Note:  This could also happen with the '||'  operator.

Summary: On Windows, the command syms x hangs MATLAB or takes minutes to finish.
Description: The MuPAD kernel can take several minutes or longer to initialize on Windows. Any command from the Symbolic Math Toolbox that requires starting MuPAD might seem to hang.
Workaround: The attached zip file contains the instructions and libraries needed to change R2008b or R2009a to create an in-process MuPAD kernel for symbolic computations. Download the file and extract it to begin the installation.

Summary: On some Linux systems, starting MATLAB with -r switch may cause erroneous shopt warning message to appear
Description: On some Linux distributions, starting MATLAB with the -r switch may cause the following erroneous message to appear: path-to-matlab : 674: shopt: not found  Beyond this warning message, there should be no ill effects. ?

Summary: Cannot use mex -inline option to compile C MEX-files containing calls to mxGetChars in MATLAB Version 7.7 (R2008b)
Description: When using "mex -inline" to compile C code containing a call to mxGetChars , the code fails to compile. For example, if you type: mex -inline mex_file.c MATLAB displays a message similar to: mex_file.cmex_file.c(7) : error C2039: 'get_chars' : is not a member of 'mxArray_tag'        INSTALL-PATH\extern\include\matrix.h(398) : see declaration of 'mxArray_tag'  INSTALL-PATH\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Compile of 'mex_file.c' failed.
Workaround: In any file that calls mxGetChars, add the line "#undef mxGetChars" after the line containing the "mex.h" include file. This turns off ARRAY_ACCESS_INLINING for mxGetChars only. For example:#include "mex.h"#undef mxGetChars...

Summary: POLYVAL function errors out when used with Galois Field (GF) arrays created with non-default primitive polynomials
Description: When POLYVAL function is used with Galois Field (GF) arrays, it may error out with the following error message: ??? Error using ==> gf.times at 16 Primitive polynomials must match. This error occurs if the arguments of the POLYVAL are two GF arrays created with a non-default primitive polynomial. The following code shows an example of this error, where the arguments of POLYVAL, a and gfPoly, are two GF arrays with primitive polynomial D^8+D^7+D^2+D+1 (391 decimal). a = gf(2,8,391); gfPoly = gf(randint(1,4,256), 8,391); polyval(gfPoly, a)
Workaround: Follow these steps to edit the MATLAB file polyval.m.Quit MATLAB?Make a copy of the poly.m file in your current MATLAB installation with another name, such as polyval.m.old. This file is in MATLABROOT\toolbox\comm\comm\@gf, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt.Find the following code (line 27):y = gf(zeros(m,n), p.m);Replace this line with the following code:y = gf(zeros(m,n), p.m, p.prim_poly);Save the fileRestart MATLABIssue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Segmentation violation when using polybool function on challenging geometry
Description: A segmentation violation may result when you apply function polybool  to a polygon with "challenging" geometry. Examples of this geometry include duplicate vertices, pairs of partially-overlapping edges, and self intersections.
Workaround: The R2007b version of Mapping Toolbox improved the robustness of the polybool function. See Bug Report 377456. Upgrading to R2007b or a more recent release will eliminate some problems. In addition, you can try to pre-process your polygons with either of the following two operations, if possible or permissible in your application: (1) remove duplicate vertices and/or (2) perturb the vertex locations with a small amount of random noise. For help with these operations, you can use the adjustPolygonVertices function provided in the attachment.

Summary: Segmentation violation after editing character data in a uitable in MATLAB 7.7 (R2008b)
Description: After repeated edits to character data in a uitable object in MATLAB 7.7 (R2008b), a MATLAB segmentation violation can occur.  The issue can be triggered using the following steps: 1. Start MATLAB and enter the following commands: tmp = {''}; t = uitable('ColumnEditable',true,'Data',tmp);   2. Edit the value in the table cell and then click somewhere else. 3. Close the figure. 4. Recreate the table and perform steps 2 and 3 until segmentation violation occurs (this may take a few iterations).  ?

Summary: A sub-package directory name containing a dot can cause MATLAB to crash or produce incorrect results.
Description: If you have a nested package directory in the form /a/+b/+c.bak , changing the current directory to the sub-package directory +c.bak  can cause MATLAB to crash, in releases R2008a and R2008b. In R2009a, the same directory naming would not cause a crash, but the number of packages displayed by executing meta.package.fromName('a')  might be wrong.
Workaround: Do not use package names that contain dots (.).

Summary: Simulink System Loop component incorrectly orders sections when numbering sections by system hierarchy
Description: The System Loop component incorrectly orders the sections when these options are selected: "Number sections by system hierarchy" Section option Any Sort Systems option except "By traversal order"  For example, if System Loop is set to number sections by system hierarchy and sort systems alphabetically, the sections would appear in the wrong order, such as: 1. aaa1.2. eee1.3. ggg 1.1. bbb1.1.1. ccc1.1.2. ddd1.2.1 fff instead of: 1. aaa1.1. bbb1.1.1. ccc1.1.2. ddd1.2. eee1.2.1 fff1.3. ggg
Workaround: To work around this issue, try setting the System Loop component's Sort Systems option to "By traversal order."

Summary: Certain operations that implicitly assign to ans do not display the correct result.
Description: Certain operations that implicitly assign to the ans  variable and that also result in a nop (i.e., the output is the same as the input) have been seen to display results incorrectly.In this example, MATLAB is expected to assign the output of sum to the ans  variable:   x = pi;   sum(x)   x =            % this should be 'ans ='       3.1416 Note that this is a cosmetic bug only.  No data is lost or incorrectly assigned.  Only the output is different.
Workaround: Disable the JIT at the command prompt with the command:   feature jitallow prompt off.

Error opening project: unpack() argument 2 must be string or read-only buffer, not list.   ?

Summary: Counter Limited or Counter Free-Running blocks feeding a Sum block might throw a segmentation violation if users do not have a Simulink Fixed Point license
Description: Simulink will throw a segmentation violation if the following conditions are met. A Counter Limited or Counter Free-Running block feeds a Sum block The Data Type Propagation block contained in the Counter Limited or Counter Free-Running block attempts to register a fixed-point data type There is no Simulink Fixed Point license installed

Summary: Real-Time Windows Target models that contain S-Function Builder blocks fail to build
Description: Real-Time Windows Target models that contain S-Function Builder blocks fail to build because the Real-Time Windows Target software cannot access the generated code from the S-Function Builder block.
Workaround: As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files. Summary: Release number incorrect for R2009a version of MATLAB Builder NE
Description: The version number listed in the documentation for MATLAB Builder NE states the release number is 3.1 for version R2009a of the product. In fact, the version number is 3.0.1.
Workaround: No workaround available.

Summary: Faulty interaction between horzcat, end, and dot selection
Description: In certain constructions, horzcat  (or the square brackets operator), end , and dot-selection mistakenly produce comma-separated lists rather than an array.  For example, the code shown here should return the the vector [1 2 3].  Instead, it yields a comma-separated list:  s.f = {1 2 3};  [s(1).f{1:end}]  ans =     1  ans =     2  ans =     3
Workaround: To work around this problem, use length (or size) instead of end:  [s(1).f{1:length(s(1).f)}]  ans =     1     2     3

Summary: Videos for SimEvents debugger demo do not play from the help browser.

Summary: Possible MATLAB crash if you close SimMechanics visualization window while running simulation
Description: If you close the SimMechanics visualization window while a simulation is running, you can cause MATLAB to crash. Such crashes are sporadic and not consistent, but more likely to occur if AVI recording is enabled.
