Summary: Axes XColor and ZColor values fail to restore properly when changed back to RGB from Gray Scale in a Print Preview
Description: In the Print Preview window, when you change the Color Scale (on the Color  tab) back to RGB  from Gray Scale , the values of the XColor  and ZColor  properties do not restore. The XColor  value remains set to its Gray Scale value, and the XColor  value is set to the original XColor  value. figure;plot(1:10)set(gca,'ZColor',[1 0 0], 'YColor',[0 1 0], 'XColor',[0 0 1]);  view([45 45]);printpreview In the Print Preview window display, observe that the vertical lines (the 'Z' dimension) are colored red. Now click on the Color  tab on the left side of the Print Preview window and click on the Gray Scale  Color Scale radio button. All lines change to either black or gray. Then, click on the RBG  Color Scale radio button and observe that the vertical lines are not restored to their original red color; they remain gray. The colors for the 'X' dimension are also not restored to their original values. ?
Workaround: Save the values of the XColor and ZColor properties before opening the Print Preview window:origColors = get(gca, {'XColor', 'ZColor'});printpreview;Restore the values when switching back to RGB or after closing the Print Preview window:set(gca, {'XColor', 'ZColor'}, origColors);?

Summary: Removing a subsystem connected to a Scope Viewer with the Undo button, and then running a Simulink model causes a segmentation violation.
Description: This problem is limited to the Scope Viewers you create using the contextual menu Create & Connect Viewer > Simulink > Scope. In a Simulink model, select some blocks and create a subsystem. Connect a Scope Viewer to the subsystem output. Use the Undo button to remove the subsystem. Simulate the model.A segmentation violation occurs and MATLAB closes.
Workaround: After removing a subsystem with the Undo button, but prior to running the model, delete the Scope Viewer. To delete the Scope Viewer,Right-click the output signal, and from the contextual menu, select Signal & Scope Manager.In the Signal & Scope Manager dialog and from the viewer pane, select the scope.Click the X button to delete.

Summary: Generating code for a subsystem can cause incorrect data type display
Description: After you generate code for a subsystem (by choosing Real-Time Workshop > Build subsystem  from the Tools  menu or the context menu) the data types displayed on signals within the subsystem may not match the data types specified where the signals originate. This is only a display issue: simulation results and generated code are unaffected.
Workaround: Update the diagram. The update restores display of the correct signal data types.

Summary: Connecting two or more viewer scopes to signals located in the same model reference may cause viewer scopes to get disconnected when the model is saved and reloaded.
Description: This problem occurs when two or more viewer scopes are connected to signals that originate from the same model reference,  loaded from a library.  The problem also occurs if such signals are displayed on multiple axes of a single viewer scope. The data is saved correctly to the model file. However, when reloading from the model from file, the first occurence of a signal from the model reference reloads from the library, and subsequent signals force multiple library reloads.  Any reload after the first one resets the signal names, and some viewer scopes are now listed as "not connected".
Workaround: Several possible workarounds exist:Connect a single viewer per submodel.Use Scope blocks instead of viewers.

Summary: impoly context menu becomes inactive when setPosition method used to alter number of vertices in polygon
Description: The setPosition  method of impoly  can be used to alter the number of vertices in an instance of impoly . For example: h_fig = figure; imshow('gantrycrane.png'); h_poly = impoly(gca, [188,30; 189,142; 93,141]); h_poly.setPosition([188,30; 189,142; 93,141; 13,41]);  Note that after the call to setPosition , all of the vertex context menus become inactive. (Nothing happens if you right click over a vertex.) The context menus of any additional lines between vertices are also inactive.

Summary: Adding or removing line branches can cause signal labels to be repositioned
Description: Adding branches to signal lines that already display a signal label may cause new signal labels to appear in unexpected locations on the branched signal. Deleting branches of these multiply labeled lines can also cause the labels to be repositioned.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'Level 1 Software Safety Analysis' with the message: '@FFT: variable <name> already declared'
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on Ada code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: **** Level 1 Software Safety Analysis - 1 (Analyzing <package>.atz)***** Level 1 Software Safety Analysis - 1.1 (Interprocedural static verification)  ...----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: variable <name> is already declared.An internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,the computation has been halted.Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support sitehttps://www.mathworks.com/support.Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.----------------------------------------------------------------------FATAL ERROR while processing <package>.dfaexception Failure(Failure) raised.FATAL ERROR  This problem occurs due to a variable declaration in the body of a task bewteen two 'accept ' with the same name.
Workaround: Moving variable declaration elsewhere in the task could resolve the issue.

Summary: Interaction between eval'ed functions handles, save, clear, and load leads to a seg-fault.
Description: Consider the MATLAB file test.m  shown below:    function h = test   h = eval('@(x)x'); The following operations entered at the command line result in a seg-fault:    >> h = test;   >> save testfile h   >> clear h   >> load testfile   >> clear test   >> h(0) If these same commands are executed from within a function instead of at the command line, you are likely to get the same result.

Summary: Multiple calls to AUTOSAR RTE function for an explicit read do not return consistent values in a time step.
Description: When using the AUTOSAR target, a top-level inport maps to a call to the AUTOSAR RTE. If the top-level inport maps to an explicit read, and the inport has duplicates, then the function that executes the explicit read for the inport (and its duplicates) is called multiple times. Since the function performing the explicit read does not guarantee consistency of value throughout the time step, the return value of one function call may differ from that of another function call in the same time step. This is not the desired functionality.

Summary: Simulink HDL Coder generates illegal Verilog code for Multiport Switch block having vector data inputs and a scalar control input.
Description: Simulink HDL Coder generates illegal Verilog code for the Multiport Switch block in cases where the block has vector data inputs and a scalar control input. The coder generates a single conditional assignment statement that switches among vectors. This is illegal in Verilog (although it is legal in VHDL).
Workaround: To work around this problem, use Switch blocks to replace the Multiport Switch when you have vector data inputs and a scalar control input.Another way to avoid this problem is to set the target language to VHDL instead of Verilog.

Summary: The wait bar in the function nlfilter reaches completion before the operation is complete
Description: The wait bar in the function nlfilter  reaches completion before the operation is complete, particularly when there are many more rows than columns in the image.
Workaround: Edit nlfilter.m and change the call to waitbar from waitbar(i/na) to waitbar(i/ma).

Summary: The Asynchronous Machine block does not work in phasor mode when the Mechanical input parameter is set to speed w
Description: If you do a phasor simulation of an Asynchronous Machine block with its mechanical input parameter set to speed w in phasor mode, you get an error message similar to: Invalid dimensions encountered while propagating dimensions from output port 2 of 'power_asm_sat/Asynchronous Machine with saturation/Electrical model/Phasor/Positive Sequence Model' to input port 3 of 'power_asm_sat/Asynchronous Machine with saturation/Electrical model/Phasor/Mux3'. During propagation, we are attempting to unify the dimensions of the multiplexed signal with dimensions {[-1],[-1]} to obtain the dimensions [-1].
Workaround: To work around this issue:1. Download the attached file spsAsynchronousMachineModel.mdl.2. Copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.3. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: User navigation is not preserved when figures are docked or undocked
Description: When a virtual reality figure (vrfigure) is docked or undocked, user navigation is not preserved. As a result, the camera reverts to the most recent viewpoint. You cannot revert to the position that the camera was in before docking or undocking.

Summary: bintprog sometimes incorrectly report that a problem is infeasible.
Description: Sometimes bintprog  incorrectly reports that a problem is infeasible, when in fact, feasible points exist. The solver claims infeasibility even if you try different values of the TolRLPFun  option (termination tolerance on the function value of a linear programming relaxation problem). This error is due to a bug in the feasibility phase, also known as Phase 1, of the simplex method.

Summary: The GUIDE Layout Editor incorrectly displays invisible uicontrols and uicontrols within invisible containers
Description: When opening an existing GUIDE GUI for editing, popupmenus can display incorrectly in the layout area or not at all, especially when selected. This can happen if a popupmenu is invisible or a popupmenu is in a panel hierarchy that is invisible (i.e., the control or a container's Visible  property is set to 'off' ).  Nevertheless, you can still select the uicontrol in the GUIDE Layout Editor or Object Browser and make changes to it. You can also save and run your GUI correctly. ?
Workaround: If you are running MATLAB R2008b, to work around this issue, download the attached files, guidefunc.m and hgload.m, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following files. MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\guide\guidefunc.mMATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\graphics\hgload.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: guidefunc.m.oldDownload the attached files, guidefunc.m and hgload.m, storing them in the directories specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'C++ source normalization' with the message: '@FFT: get_entity failed on subtree'
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C++ code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: **** Gnu C++ source normalization 3 (P_VRF) **** Gnu C++ source normalization 4 (P_MDF)----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: get_entity failed on subtreeAn internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,the computation has been halted.Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support sitehttps://www.mathworks.com/support.Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.----------------------------------------------------------------------***** Gnu C++ source normalization 4.1 (P_TRM)***** Gnu C++ source normalization 4.1 (P_TRM)... exception Match(Match) raised. This problem occurs due to the cast of a pointer to member functions on constants.

Summary: Calling bsxfun with certain types of sparse matrices may crash MATLAB?.
Description: The way that bsxfun  handles certain sparse matrices may cause MATLAB? to terminate. If the first column of the sparse matrix output has a significantly smaller number of nonzeros than the rest of the matrix, MATLAB? will crash.
Workaround: Convert the sparse matrices to full before calling bsxfun.

Summary: For Discrete Integrator in algebraic loop, warning is incorrect if the loop does not pass through reset or IC port.
Description: In Simulink R14FCS and later, if a Discrete Integrator resides in an algebraic loop, Simulink throws a warning "Detected algebraic loop path(s) through integrator reset or IC ports. ...", even if the loop passes through the derivate input port rather than the reset or IC port. Under such circumstances, this warning is incorrect.
Workaround: No workaround exists.

Summary: Memory leak when calling a nested function.
Description: In some cases, calling a function that itself calls a nested function may cause MATLAB to leak memory.
Workaround: Run the following command to prevent the leak from happening:feature jitallow fast_jit_call off??

Summary: Kinetic Damping Actuator block in the library mech_friction.mdl errors out when attached to a revolute primitive
Description: If you connect the Kinetic Damping Actuator block from the mech_friction.mdl  library to a revolute primitive, simulating or updating the model stops with an error: Primitive P1 was not found on the Joint. Select a legal primitive to actuate in the Joint Actuator dialog.
Workaround: Close the mech_friction.mdl model file if it is open.Back up the mech_friction.mdl file in the $matlabroot\toolbox\physmod\mech\mechdemos directory. Renaming it to mech_friction_old.mdl is recommended.$matlabroot is the directory returned by the command matlabroot.Download and place the files attached to this report in the $matlabroot\toolbox\physmod\mech\mechdemos directory.The workaround is applicable for R2006b and later versions.

Summary: Requirements linking does not work with Microsoft Office 2007 documents.
Description: In releases prior to R2009b, if you try to create a link to Microsoft Office 2007 document using one of the new file extensions (.docx, .xlsx, etc.), two problems occur: The Link Settings dialog box refuses to accept the document name. Selection-based linking fails.
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by modifying your installation. You need to manually modify the following files:toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/private/linktype_rmi_word.mtoolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/private/linktype_rmi_excel.mIn these two files, find the line that starts withlinktype.Extensions =and modify the right-hand side to assign the following supported extensions, correspondingly:{'.doc', '.rtf', '.docx', '.docm'};and{'.xls', '.csv', '.xlsx', '.xlsm', '.xlsb'};?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Math functions involving literal integer constants use single precision
Description: Starting in R2007b (up to and including R2009a), math functions such as sin, cos, exp and the power operator (^) started using single-precision versions of the functions (for simulation and code generation) when they are passed literal integer constants. Consider the attached model answer_diff.mdl. In this model, a Stateflow chart and an Embedded MATLAB Function block both compute the result of 10^0.1. If you simulate the model in R2007a or previous versions, you will see that the output from the Embedded MATLAB Function block and the Stateflow chart match exactly. In R2007b, you will see a small difference in the two computations of 10^0.1 because: The Embedded MATLAB Function block uses the pow function. The Stateflow chart uses the powf function.  Starting in R2009b, the rule for choosing the single-precision or double-precision version of a math function is: Stateflow chooses the double-precision version of a math function unless it finds a variable of type single passed as the first argument to the math function. This difference affects only models that use literal integer constants. Expressions with variables of type double always use the double-precision versions of the math functions.
Workaround: To use double-precision math functions, verify that the first argument to the math functions (or the ^ operator) is of type double.For example, instead of using 10^0.1, please use 10.0^0.1.

Summary: A red NIV on an array is not detected when using the main generator
Description: PolySpace for Ada 95 does not identify all NIV errors for arrays and parameters. When using the main generator, verification does not detect a red NIV. A red NIV is detected if you specify -main A.P The following example (in 3 files) reproduces the problem. A.ada : -------------------- package A is procedure P (X : out INTEGER); end A; package body A is type ARRAY_T is array (1..2) of INTEGER; procedure Q (INPUT : in ARRAY_T; OUTPUT : out INTEGER) is separate; procedure P (X : out INTEGER) is separate; end A; ------------------- P.ada : separate (A) procedure P (X : out INTEGER)is V : ARRAY_T; begin Q (INPUT => V, OUTPUT => X); end P; ----------------------- Q.ada : procedure Q (INPUT : in ARRAY_T; OUTPUT : out INTEGER)is begin OUTPUT := INPUT(1); end Q; ?

Summary: _INTERFACE_INFO structure definition in mclmcr.h conflicts with structure in ws2ipdef.h
Description: A name conflict causes a compilation failure when using C/C++ shared libraries developed with MATLAB Compiler. The conflict is between a definition in a MathWorks-provided header file with a type definition in a Microsoft system header file. The MATLAB Compiler-shipped header files must be included into application source code. These headers utilize a shared library built with MATLAB Compiler. One such header file, mclmcr.h , includes a type declaration for an _INTERFACE_INFO data structure. A particular Microsoft header file, ws2ipdef.h , includes a type declaration for a data structure with the same name. If an application is built that includes ws2ipdef.h , and this application also includes the header file generated by MATLAB Compiler for a shared library, a compilation error results, indicating a duplicate definition of this structure. This only occurs on Microsoft Windows.
Workaround: This workaround is for MATLAB Compiler 4.8. Download the attached mclmcr.h file to MATLABROOT\extern\include if using mcc/deploytool or MCRRROOT\extern\include if building a shared library on a machine without MATLAB.

Summary: Including the XCP Server block in a model that uses other communications causes a send overrun error
Description: Building and downloading a target application that includes the XCP Server block in a model that works with other heavy network traffic might display the following send overrun error on the target PC monitor: error send overrun, r=1, c=0 The connection between the Vector CANape and xPC Target systems will subsequently close. This error is sporadic and not always reproducible.

Summary: Silent wrong results with SimState save and restore feature
Description: Some Signal Processing Blockset blocks do not support the Simulink SimState  save and restore feature, but do not appropriately error out. When you restart a simulation with the initial state loaded from the final state of the previous run, these blocks do not give the same results that they would have if only one simulation had been run. Instead of informing you of this issue, the blocks silently give incorrect numerical results. The affected blocks are: Buffer Cumulative Product Cumulative Sum Fast Block LMS Filter From Multimedia File From Wave File Inverse Short-Time FFT Mean Overlap-Add FFT Filter Overlap-Save FFT Filter Queue RLS Filter RMS Short-Time FFT Signal From Workspace (in cyclic repetition mode with frame-based signals) Spectrum Scope (when the input is buffered) Stack Standard Deviation To Multimedia File To Wave File Variance
Workaround: Do not use the listed blocks with the Simulink SimState feature to save the complete simulation state, end the simulation, then restart the simulation at the point where you ended.

Summary: FFT Analysis Tool of Powergui does not work correctly for signals not sampled at fixed rate
Description: When you open the FFT Analysis Tool from Powergui, the FFT window does not display variable-step sampled signals.
Workaround: The workaround depends on your product version.For R2009a:1. Download the attached files power_fftscope.m and power_fftscope_pr.p.2. Copy the file power_fftscope.m into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.3. Copy the file power_fftscope_pr.p into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/private/4. Restart MATLAB.For earlier versions:1. Download one of the attached files, depending on your product version:- For R2008a, download the file R2008a_power_fftscope.p.- For R2008b, download the file R2008b_power_fftscope.p.2. Rename the file to power_fftscope.p.3. Copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.4. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Why do I get incorrect Lagrange multipliers when using the fmincon interior-point algorithm on problems with fixed variables?
Description: When using the fmincon interior-point  algorithm on a problem that has fixed variables (equal lower and upper bounds for some components), the solver returns an incorrect Lagrange multiplier for the corresponding lower or upper bound. One of the corresponding Lagrange multipliers should be zero, but neither are zero.

Summary: Using the function mldivide on a nearly singular real symmetric indefinite matrix may sometimes cause MATLAB? to hang.
Description: Using the function mldivide  on a nearly singular real symmetric indefinite matrix may sometimes cause MATLAB? to hang. If this happens, MATLAB? uses 100% of CPU load and you cannot use Ctrl+C to interrupt.

Summary: When printed or exported to PDF, a MuPAD notebook with mathematical expressions or graphics can have deficiencies at page breaks
Description: When printing a MuPAD notebook containing graphics, typesetted formulas, or other inline objects, these objects can be splitted at page breaks or printed twice on two pages or both. The objects can be printed outside the page area in the top or bottom margin of the page. The same behavior holds for PDF export.

Summary: Simulink Data Class Designer deletes extra files inside class directories
Description: When writing out changes, the Simulink Data Class Designer deletes certain files that it did not originally create. For example, if you check your @package  directory into a revision control system and the revision control system adds extra directories or files, the Data Class Designer will delete them.
Workaround: The following installation instructions may be used to apply a patch provided in the Attachments section below to your MATLAB installation to prevent this issue from occurring.Note: The attached version of sldataclassdesigner.p will not work in R2008a or earlier releases.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>toolbox\simulink\simulink folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>toolbox\simulink\simulink folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>toolbox\simulink\simulinkunzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>toolbox\simulink\simulink folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Setting a negative value for the Lm parameter of a Mutual Inductance block can produce an error
Description: Specifying a negative value for the Lm term in a Mutual Inductance block can, depending on the circuit topology, potentially lead to an error message similar to the following: Invalid setting in 'mutualdemo/powergui/EquivalentModel1/State-Space' for parameter 'A'.

Summary: Unnecessary type casts for Logical Operator (XOR) block in generated code
Description: When boolean signals connect to a Logical Operator block, with Operator  set to XOR , the generated code might have unnecessary type casts to uint8 . The generated code for this block might appear as follows: (boolean_T)(int8_T)((int8_T)((int8_T)In1 ^ (int8_T)In2) ^ (int8_T)In3)

Summary: Using the Watcom compiler to build models containing Ethernet blocks causes run time errors.
Description: Using the Ethernet or Network Buffer blocks with the Watcom compiler generates initialization errors on the target. The error message displayed contains a large meaningless error number. This bug only exisits when running a model that contains Ethenet blocks built with the Watcom compiler.
Workaround: Use one of the supported Visual compilers to build the model.

Summary: Pseudoinverse block yields NaN outputs for all-zero inputs.
Description: If the input to the Pseudoinverse block is an all-zero matrix, the output is all NaNs.

Summary: The page orientation options in the Print dialog box are disabled after installing Java for OS X 10.5 Update 2
Description: After installing Java for OS X 10.5 Update 2, the Orientation controls in the Page Setup tab of the Print dialog are disabled, and, therefore you are unable to change the orientation between portrait and landscape. This occurs when trying to print from the Command Window, Editor, figures, etc.

Summary: On Linux platforms, models that contain the Joystick Input block might fail if you have not connected the joystick to the model
Description: On Linux platforms, models that contain the Joystick Input block might fail if you have not connected the joystick to the model.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: generated VxWorks example main function might incorrectly call custom step function or method for multirate model with continuous states
Description: A generated VxWorks example main function might incorrectly call a custom step function or method, if all of the following conditions are true: Model is a multirate, single-tasking system and has continuous states. Model step function or method is customized with one of the following: C function prototype C++ encapsulation interface that uses the I/O arguments step method  function specification.   At least one argument of the step function or method uses the Pointer  category.  When deployed on a VxWorks platform, the code potentially can cause a fatal kernel task-level exception.

Summary: Two devices sharing the same output scan clock may lose synchronization
Description: When you use two Measurement Computing devices and export the output scan clock of one device to be used as the output scan clock of the other, the signals are synchronized in the beginning. The devices will then lose synchronization even though they ought to stay synchronized throughout the entire generation. This example shows the problem when you view the output data on a scope. % Create the analog output objects and add a channel to eachao1=analogoutput('mcc',1); % In our testing this was a MCC USB-2537ao2=analogoutput('mcc',2); % In our testing this was a MCC USB-1616HS-2addchannel(ao1,0);addchannel(ao2,0);    % Configure the clock sources% Note that in MCC's InstaCal you must configure the controlling device output % scan clock to output its signal.    % The next line is for clarity as the default value for the 'ClockSource' property is 'Internal'set(ao1,'ClockSource','Internal');    % Set the controlled device's clock source to external so it will use the controlling deviceˇŻs clock instead of its own  set(ao2,'ClockSource','External');    % Create the data and put it to the devices T=10; % duration of generation fs=1e5; % sample rate t=(1:fs*T)'/fs; data=sin(2*pi*1000*t);     set([ao1 ao2],'samplerate',fs);putdata(ao1,data);   % Amplify one signal slightly to make the offset more visibleputdata(ao2,data * 1.1);   % Start the objects and wait for the generation to complete% Watch the signals on the scope and notice that they get out of syncstart([ao1 ao2]);wait([ao1 ao2], (T * 1.1) + 1) % Wait 10% plus one second longer than the duration   % Clean updelete([ao1 ao2])   ?
Workaround: To work around this issue use the analog output object's BufferingConfig property to force the size of the buffer to hold at least one second's worth of data. Set the number of buffers so that BufferSize*NumberOfBuffers is large enough to hold all the generated data.Add this line before calling putdata:set([ao1 ao2], 'BufferingConfig', [fs T]);

Summary: Generation of data stops prematurely when you use the clock signal of another device
Description: When you use two Measurement Computing devices and export the analog output scan clock of one device to be used as the analog output scan clock of the second, the second device stops generating data before the first stops. They should stop simultaneously.This example shows the problem when you view the output data on a scope. % Create the analog output objects and add a channel to each% ao1 is the first device and exports its clock signalao1=analogoutput('mcc',1); % For our testing this was a MCC USB-2537   % ao2 is the second device and is using the clock signal from ao1ao2=analogoutput('mcc',2); % For our testing this was a MCC USB-1616HS-2addchannel(ao1,0);addchannel(ao2,0);   % Note that in MCC's InstaCal you must configure the controlling device's output% scan clock to output its signal.  % The next line is for clarity as the default value for the 'ClockSource'% property is 'Internal'set(ao1,'ClockSource','Internal');  % Set the controlled device's clock source to external so it will use % the controlling device's clock instead of its owns et(ao2,'ClockSource','External');  % Create the data and put it to the devicesT=2; % duration of generationfs=1e5; % sample ratet=(1:fs*T)'/fs;data=sin(2*pi*1000*t);  set([ao1 ao2],'samplerate',fs);  % Note: for workaround replace the next two lines. See Workaround.putdata(ao1,data);putdata(ao2,data);% Start the objects and wait for the generation to complete% Watch the signals on the scope and notice that the second (controlled) device stops % before the first (controlling) device  start([ao1 ao2]);wait([ao1 ao2], (T * 1.1) + 1) % Wait 10% plus one second longer than the duration   % clean updelete([ao1 ao2])  ?
Workaround: To ensure that the second device generates the entire signal, pad the two devices' output data with zeros as shown:putdata(ao1,[ data ; zeros(1e5, 1)]);putdata(ao2,[ data ; zeros(1e5, 1)]);

Summary: Japanese Student Edition: Searching in the Function Browser usually fails to return results
Description: In the Japanese language Student Edition, searching for functions in the Function Browser using Japanese text will usually not return any search results. "No search results found" appears instead, even when a match exists. For example, searching for the Japanese translation of plot  in the Function Browser does not return the MATLAB plot  function.
Workaround: Use an English language search, or browse for the function by category in the tree view.

Summary: RTDX? fails to write messages to C6000? simulators with Code Composer Studio? version 3.3, SRs 8 through 11
Description: RTDX host-to-target message transfers do not work on C6000 simulators with CCS 3.3 Service Releases 8, 9, 10, and 11. The Embedded IDE Link CC? function writemsg  fails to pass messages to the processor. Therefore, the RTDX demos "RTDX Tutorial" and "Comparing Simulation and Target Implementation with RTDX" do not work with these CCS configurations. You can reproduce this problem in CCS IDE by running the host-to-target RTDX diagnostic test using the following steps: Select Tools > RTDX > Diagnostic Control  from the menu bar in the IDE. This opens the Diagnostic Control dialog box. Select Host-To-Target Test  in the Diagnostic Control dialog box to run the host-to-target diagnostic test.  ? ? ?

Summary: Activation results in failures on newer Linux distributions
Description: When you start MATLAB and run the Activation application, the following errors occur for certain configurations: MATLAB: xcb_xlib.c:52: xcb_xlib_unlock:Assertion `c->xlib.lock' failed. Abort The errors occur for any MATLAB version that uses Sun Microsystems Java software version 1.5 or 1.6, on a newer Linux distribution, such as Fedora 7, that has updated xcb and xlib libraries (Xserver 1.4 and X.Org 7.3). This is the topic of bug report 6532373, issued by Sun Microsystems. ?
Workaround: You can use one of these workarounds:Install Sun Microsystems JRE software version 1.6.0_10, or higher, for the Linux operating system. Then set the MATLAB_JAVA environment variable to point to that installation. For more information, see "Using a Different Version of JVM Software" in the MATLAB External Interfaces documentation (https://www.mathworks.com/access/helpdesk/help/techdoc/matlab_external/f98533.html#f122001). Note that MATLAB has not been qualified with JRE version 1.6.0_10 software, although no issues are anticipated.You can avoid the error by setting the variable LIBXCB_ALLOW_SLOPPY_LOCK=1, or by modifying libawt.so with the following command:   sed -i 's/XINERAMA/FAKEEXTN/g' <path to libawt.so>?

Summary: Multi-port Switch blocks loaded from Simulink library files can occasionally be assigned the wrong number of ports.
Description: If a Simulink library created in R2007b or earlier contains a Multi-port Switch block with 4 inputs, then under some conditions in R2008a and later, that block may be assigned only 3 inputs when it is loaded. This is caused by an interaction between the "BlockParameterDefaults" section of the MDL file, and incremental loading, in which only the required portions of Simulink libraries are loaded at any one time. Under some conditions the "BlockParameterDefaults" information can be lost by the block diagram, and when further portions of the library are loaded, they inherit the wrong defaults. The conditions that cause this are: Encountering a "new" block type (i.e. one that hasn't been used so far in the Simulink session) in an MDL file AND, having in memory a partially loaded Simulink library, last saved in R2007b or earlier, which contains in its unloaded portion a Multi-port Switch block with 3 inputs AND then loading that block from the library
Workaround: You can work around this problem by closing the library and then Simulink will reload it. Enter:>> bdclose(mylibrary)It may be necessary to "update" your model (press Ctrl+D) to see the changes take effect.To prevent these conditions occurring in the first place, explicitly load any libraries that contain Multi-port Switch blocks before anything else. Enter:>> load_system(mylibrary)This command ensures that the entire library is loaded into memory, which means that the above conditions cannot occur.

Summary: Printing figures from their File menu on non-Windows platforms can generate a Java exception in MATLAB.
Description: When you create a figure and select Print  from the figure File  menu, you can receive a stack trace beginning with the following entries in the Command Window: java.lang.NullPointerException: null attributeat sun.print.IPPPrintService.isAttributeValueSupported(Unknown Source)at sun.print.ServiceDialog$OrientationPanel.updateInfo(Unknown Source)at sun.print.ServiceDialog$PageSetupPanel.updateInfo(Unknown Source)at sun.print.ServiceDialog.updatePanels(Unknown Source) This is a bug in how some versions of Java interact with the CUPS printing service. An incompatibility exists between CUPS versions 1.3.4 and later and Java versions earlier than Java 1.6 update 10 creates a Java exception when trying to print MATLAB figures. To check which version of Java is being used run the following MATLAB command: version -java
Workaround: See Technical Solution 1-747TRF on The MathWorks web site.

Summary: The 'reuse' and 'replace' options of the open_system command fail for some destination system names
Description: The 'reuse'  and 'replace'  options of the open_system  command fail for destination system names that are truncated versions of other open_system  options, such as the 'browse'  option. For example, the following command fails: open_system('b/ss', 'b', 'reuse').

Summary: A pixel "trail" may be left on a model when dragging a block on a Windows machine.
Description: Setting a block's AttributesFormatString  to a value that results in no output (e.g., "\n", "%" with an empty description) can cause a pixel "trail" to remain after dragging the block across a model window on some Microsoft Windows configurations. In other words, as you drag an item, a trail of unerased pixels remains between the old position of the item and the new position.  This has no effect on the model or on simulation.
Workaround: Any action that refreshes the model window, such as changing the zoom factor, also clears the "trail."

Summary: MATLAB Compiler does not pick up class static methods that have an external implementation
Description: When compiling code that uses a MATLAB class with a static method, where that method is implemented in a MATLAB file outside the class definition file, the MATLAB Compiler fails to include the static method implementation. Attempts to call the static method in compiled code will then fail. For example, Given the class foo  implementated in the directory @foo  with the class definition file @foo/foo.m : classdef foo   methods(Static)     mymethod;   end end and the M file @foo/mymethod.m : function mymethod  disp 'hi'; an attempt to call foo.mymethod  in compiled code will fail. This failure occurs in the MATLAB Compiler and in all of the Builder products that use MATLAB compiler.
Workaround: This problem can be avoided by implementing the static method in the class definition file. The example in the bug description can be reimplemented as:classdef foo  methods(Static)    function mymethod       disp 'hi';    end   end endThis implementation works as expected.

Summary: On Microsoft Windows Vista? platforms, deleting files and performing related actions in protected folders will fail without notice
Description: On 32-bit, Microsoft Windows Vista? platforms, file operations you perform in protected folders that involve a delete or write action fail without notification. The actions appear to have succeeded. For example, if you use the Current Directory browser to delete a file from C:\Program Files , which is a protected folder, the file is not deleted. But you are not notified that the delete operation failed. It appears that the delete operation succeeded. Similarly, if you copy a file and paste it into a protected folder, and a file with the same name already exists, the paste operation fails. You are informed that the existing file was successfully overwritten by the pasted file. When you look at the results of your actions using Windows Vista Explorer, you can see that the actions failed. This problem occurs if you have User Account Control (UAC) enabled in your Windows Vista environment. The problem only occurs in protected folders. It does not matter what privileges you have; for example, you have this problem even if you have administrator rights. The problem is related to a virtual access feature that Windows Vista provides.
Workaround: To perform deletions and related actions in protected folders, use Windows Vista features or operating system tools instead of the MATLAB? software. For example, use the Vista Explorer.

Summary: Startup on Linux can produce a Locking assertion failure error
Description: When you start the MATLAB? application from an operating system terminal window on a computer running Linux, error messages like the following may appear in the terminal window: Locking assertion failure. Backtrace:  #0 /usr/lib/libxcb-xlib.so.0 [0xb5410767]  #1 /usr/lib/libxcb-xlib.so.0(xcb_xlib_unlock+0x31) [0xb54108b1]  #2 /usr/lib/libX11.so.6(_XReply+0xfd) [0xb56d51bd]  #3 /d/local-debian/matlab-2008a/sys/  java/jre/glnx86/jre1.6.0/lib/i386/xawt/libmawt.so [0xaef9c2be] ... or you may see the following: MATLAB: xcb_xlib.c:52: xcb_xlib_unlock:Assertion `c->xlib.lock' failed.Abort The error messages do not appear when you start MATLAB from a remote X client or from a VNC client. After the error messages display, MATLAB may complete the startup and run without any problems. In other cases, this error prevents MATLAB from starting.
Workaround: On some platforms, the error messages do not cause any problems. You can ignore them.If you do not want the error messages to appear, or if you observe other anomalies in the MATLAB Desktop or other MATLAB windows, you can try one of the following workarounds:Set the environment variable LIBXCB_ALLOW_SLOPPY_LOCK to the value of 1. This can be done by running the following commands from a terminal: export  LIBXCB_ALLOW_SLOPPY_LOCK=1or setenv LIBXCB_ALLOW_SLOPPY_LOCK 1depending upon the shell you use. Update the libmawt.so file located in MATLAB's JRE.For 32 bit Linux, run the command: sed -i 's/XINERAMA/FAKEEXTN/g' $MATLABROOT/sys/java/jre/glnx86/$JRE/lib/i386/xawt/libmawt.soFor 64 bit Linux, run the command: sed -i 's/XINERAMA/FAKEEXTN/g' $MATLABROOT/sys/java/jre/glnxa64/$JRE/lib/amd64/xawt/libmawt.soWhere $MATLABROOT is the installation folder for MATLAB on your machine, and $JRE is the JRE folder. Refer to your installation for the exact JRE folder name. Install Sun Microsystems JRE software version 1.6.0_06 or later for the Linux operating system, where Linux is a registered trademark of Linus Torvalds. Then set the MATLAB_JAVA environment variable to point to that installation. Note that MATLAB has not been qualified with JRE version 1.6.0_06 software, although no issues are anticipated. The MathWorks currently has no plans to ship JRE version 1.6.0_06 software in a future release.

Summary: Clicking on annotations sporadically crashes Simulink on Windows XP 64bit.
Description: This is a very sporadic Simulink interface problem specific to Windows XP on a 64 bit machine, and it may not be easily observed. The problem may appear after several series of steps/actions using the Simulink interface involving editing an annotation, signal, or block label in a Simulink model by clicking on it. This activity (sometimes) leads to a Simulink crash.
Workaround: If you encounter this problem on a regular basis, and need technical assistance regarding the problem, please contact MathWorks technical support:https://www.mathworks.com/support/service_requests/contact_support.do?

Summary: Startup on Windows? platforms using the matlab command and the -minimize option gives MATLAB? focus instead of the current window
Description: When you start MATLAB on Microsoft? Windows platforms using the matlab  command and the -minimize  option, MATLAB has focus after startup instead of the current application window. You have to click the current application window to return focus to it. For example, you see this problem if you run the following statement in a DOS Command Prompt window,: matlab -nosplash -nodesktop -minimize -r "ls"

Summary: Using mask initialization scripts to switch Stateflow library charts in configurable subsystems causes crash
Description: When you use mask initialization scripts to switch Stateflow library charts in configurable subsystems, a crash occurs when you update the model. The beginning of the stack trace looks something like this: failed assertion in .\classic\cg_ctx.cpp at line 44: (((obj)!=0?((CG_ObjId)(obj)->id()):CG_BOGUS) == CG_CTX) Stack Trace:   [0] sf.mexw32:void __cdecl assertion_failed(char const *,char const *,int,char const *)(0x6dc1983c "(((obj)!=0?((CG_ObjId)(obj)->id(..", 0x6dc19824 ".\classic\cg_ctx.cpp", 44, 0) + 58 bytes   [1] sf.mexw32:void __cdecl assertion_func(void *,char const *,int,char const *)(0, 0x6dc19824 ".\classic\cg_ctx.cpp", 44, 0x6dc1983c "(((obj)!=0?((CG_ObjId)(obj)->id(..") + 22 bytes   [2] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)(0x6dc19824 ".\classic\cg_ctx.cpp", 44, 0x6dc1983c "(((obj)!=0?((CG_ObjId)(obj)->id(..", 0) + 27 bytes   [3] cg_ir.dll:class CG_Ctx * __cdecl cg_ctx(void *)(0, 10, 120, 0x00c29398) + 40 bytes   [4] sf.mexw32:void __cdecl sf_cg_get_chart_port_info(int,struct mxArray_tag * * const,int,struct mxArray_tag const * * const)(1, 0x00c29330, 0x1d4982c0, 0x00c29398) + 356 bytes
Workaround: Create an empty chart in the library model that contains the configurable subsystem.The empty chart does not affect simulation or code generation.Create a link to this library chart in the main model.Save the model.

Summary: Use of try/catch with ClassCastException results in memory leaks in the Java Runtime Environment.
Description: The use of try/catch as an alternative path of flow is discouraged by the Java Runtime Environment developers. The following code creates memory leaks in the Java VM. This has been reported to Sun. try {   //Extract vector elements   o = v.elementAt(j+c*k);   //Get primitive double   d[i][j] = ((Number)o).doubleValue(); } catch (Exception e) {   //Need dummy value to replace NULL's   d[i][j] = tmpNative;}  ?
Workaround: Replace the Java code:try {   //Extract vector elements   o = v.elementAt(j+c*k);   //Get primitive double   d[i][j] = ((Number)o).doubleValue(); } catch (Exception e) {   //Need dummy value to replace NULL's   d[i][j] = tmpNative;}with the following Java code://Extract vector elementso = v.elementAt(j+c*k);//Get primitive doubleif (o instanceof Number)  d[i][j] = ((Number)o).doubleValue();//Need dummy value to replace NULL'selse {d[i][j] = tmpNative;

Summary: Cannot deselect cells in a uitable when all are selected
Description: When all cells are selected in a uitable, it is difficult to remove the selection. For example: figure;ht = uitable('Data',magic(10), ...     'ColumnWidth',{25 25 25 25 25 25 25 25 25 25}) Drag from a corner to select every cell in the table. You cannot change or remove the selection by clicking a cell. ?
Workaround: Hold down the Ctrl key when clicking or dragging to deselect cells. Sometimes when you drag, some cells do not deselect. Ctrl+click such cells again to deselect them.

Summary: Polyspace verification stops with the message 'no space left on device' or with message 'I/O internal error in getc'
Description: When launching Polyspace verification on C code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: ****** C to intermediate language translation 1.2.3 (P_GAA_RS) ****** C to intermediate language translation 1.2.3 (P_GAA_RS) took 0real, 0u + 0s ***** C to intermediate language translation 1.2 (P_GAA_SC) took 12.1real, 10.6u + 1.5s (1.3gc) exception SysErr(OS.SysErr(name="No space left on device", syserror=nospc)) raised.  raised at: <basis>/posix/error.sml:35.30 *** I/O internal error in getc on file "#<instream>" at position 675231 exception Io(Io) raised.  raised at: /home/deutsch/alias-server.sml:628.26 **** C to intermediate language translation 1 (P_GAA3) took 54863.9real, 202.8u + 51.9s (30.4gc) Failure at: Sep 20, 2005 5:47:59 User time for polyspace-c: 55397real, 47328.1u + 1001.9s (5gc)  This problem could occur due to a complex pointer analysis inside Polyspace.

Summary: PolySpace associates '#pragma Inspection_Point' instruction to previous block
Description: When a #pragma Inspection_Point  instruction follows a statement, i.e. an if-then-else , a do-while  or a switch-case  statement, the #pragma  is attached to the last block of statement even if it is defined outside. This can be confusing especially if both branches of the statement assign variable with different values. In reallity, #pragma  has been attached to the parent tree's node list even if it looks like outside. Normally #pragma Inspection_Point  helps to understand what could be the possible values of variable ret :void main(void){  volatile int value;   int ret;   if (value > 10) {   ret = 1;  } else {   ret = 0;  }  #pragma Inspection_Point ret}  In the previous example, even if #pragma  is outside the last statement block, It gives 'ret=0'
Workaround: Add a semi-colon between the end of the statement and #pragma Inspection_Point. In the following example, a semi-colon has been added before #pragma:void main(void){ volatile int value; int ret; if (value > 10) { ret = 1; } else { ret = 0; }; #pragma Inspection_Point ret}Now #pragma gives 'ret=0 or ret=1'

Summary: MATLAB might crash if the clear all command is used within a callback while Virtual Reality Toolbox is running
Description: The following commands will unload the Virtual Reality Toolbox engine from memory: clear all  clear mex  clear functions   In such instances, Virtual Reality Toolbox must be reloaded to allow the Virtual Reality Toolbox objects to continue working. If the reload operation cannot happen immediately, MATLAB will crash. For example, calling one of these commands from the Handle Graphics object callback will cause this condition.
Workaround: As a workaround, do not unload the Virtual Reality Toolbox engine from memory while any Virtual Reality Toolbox object exists.In particular, do not call the following commands from within a Handle Graphics object callback when using Virtual Reality Toolbox:clear allclear mexclear functionsInstead, to clear all variables, use the clear command.

Summary: Linux? platform: Printing text to Postscript? printer produces blank pages
Description: On Linux? platforms, printing text from the Editor or Command Window to a Postscript? printer may produce blank pages. This is due to a bug in how Linux software converts Postscript? data into PCL (Printer Control Language).
Workaround: There are two workarounds. One is to use a PostScript printer so that there is no translation of the Postscript data. If that is not possible, follow these steps to print the content:Select File > Print > Print to File, and then click Print to create a file.Open the newly created file in a text editor.Search for the string DeferredMediaSelection, and then delete the line that contains it. For example, delete the entire line that appears similar to: <</DeferredMediaSelection true /PageSize [612.0 792.0] /ImagingBBox null /ManualFeed false /NumCopies 1 >> setpagedevice.Save the file, and then print it using a Linux? printing program (for example, KGhostView if you're using KDE).

Summary: ver command does not return MATLAB version correctly when compiled.
Description: The value returned from ver('matlab')  is incorrect in applications and shared libraries created with MATLAB Compiler or one of the Builder products. When compiled, v = ver('MATLAB')  produces the following error:  Warning: No properly formatted Contents.m file was found for'toolbox\matlab\general'.> In ver>disp_single_toolbox at 424  In ver at 81  In check_ver at 2  This function reproduces the error:  function x = check_ver    x = ver('matlab');
Workaround: You may workaround the problem by using evalc and the zero-output form of ver: function matlab_ver = check_ver    ver_info = evalc('ver(''matlab'')');    vcells = regexp(ver_info, 'MATLAB\s+\Version\s+(\d.\d)', 'tokens');    matlab_ver = vcells{2};This function returns (as a string) the first two digits of the MATLAB version number that appears on the second line of the output from the ver command.

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms: Command+Tab does not switch to MATLAB? window correctly
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms, Expos¨¦ features such as Command +Tab  do not switch to the MATLAB? window correctly; the window does not become active.
Workaround: To make the MATLAB window active, click inside the window or on its title bar.

Summary: Cannot build SimPowerSystems models that have no electrical states
Description: If you try to build a SimPowerSystems model that contains no Inductor and no Capacitor devices (electrical states), you get an error similar to the following: Error: File: MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\tlc_c\sfun_spssw_discc.tlc Line: 170 Column: 58 Undefined identifier DSTATE
Workaround: To resolve this build issue, add a small parasitic inductance or capacitance to your model. You can do this by either adding an Inductor or a Capacitor block, or modifying parameters of the blocks already existing in the model, for example, by setting Snubber capacitance to a small finite value.

Summary: MATLAB? does not respond to changes in window size or screen resolution
Description: This problem manifests itself in the following ways: If you use an external monitor or projector, for instance, and then return to your original resolution, MATLAB does not allow you to enlarge windows to the full screen size. If you change the resolution of your monitor while MATLAB is running, MATLAB behaves as if the old resolution is still in effect.
Workaround: Restart MATLAB under the new resolution.

Summary: Problem in polymerge when junctions of more than two curves exist
Description: The behavior of polymerge  is unspecified when more than two curves meet at a common point, and in some instances its behavior is clearly undesirable.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the polymerge.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/polymerge.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: polymerge.m.oldCopy the version of polymerge.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Plot blocks do not show correct results when unit conversion is on
Description: When simulating with unit conversion on, Plot blocks in the Diagram  do not show values correctly in user-defined units. Instead these values show an internal scaling of units. This does not affect the results returned from a simulation or general plotting. This is only seen in a Plot block if unit conversion is turned on for a simulation.

Summary: DEPFUN fails to locate certain types of UDD classes; some Signal Processing and Filter Design functions become undeployable as a result.
Description: If a UDD class consists of a single schema.m  file which only contains property definitions, DEPFUN will fail to locate and include that class in its list of function dependencies. Compiled applications that rely on such classes will therefore fail to run, though they will compile without error.  The Signal Processing Toolbox contains many examples of this type of class, notably abstractlpcutoffwatten , so applications that deploy code from the Signal Processing Toolbox are particularly vulnerable to this bug. This vulnerability extends to applications that use these classes indirectly, such as clients of the Filter Design Toolbox. Deployed applications that use the fdesign  class from the Filter Design Toolbox will issue the following warning at run-time:  Warning: an error occurred while parsing class fspecs.lpcutoffwatten: Invalid superclass handle.

Summary: copyobj method resets StatesToLog, and certain other configuration set properties
Description: When calling the copyobj  method on a model to create a new copy, the StatesToLog  property in every attached configuration set is reset to the default (ˇ®allˇŻ ). Therefore if you call copyobj  on a model whose active configuration set is expected to contain specific states set to log, a simulation of the copied model object returns the results for all the states in the model. Similarly, if you set the SpeciesInputFactors  or ParameterInputFactors  properties in your configuration set object these properties are not set in the copied model object.
Workaround: If you set the StatesToLog property in the configuration set of the model object then you must also set the StatesToLog in the copied model object.Similarly, if you set the SpeciesInputFactors or ParameterInputFactors properties in the configuration set of the model object then you must also set those properties in the copied model object.

Summary: The If block fails to inherit sample time from its associated action subsystem via back propagation
Description: If no sample time is specified for an If block or for the blocks driving its inputs, the If block back inherits a sample time from its associated action subsystems. However, Simulink performs the back propagation only if the sample time is specified for the blocks connected to the outputs of the action subsystems. Simulink should also perform this task when the sample time is specified for the blcoks connected to the action subsystem inputs, but it does not.

Summary: Compiler optimization and mismatch between generated and simulated code
Description: When the C24x or C28x compiler optimization is set to -o2 , the results from generated code from the Signal Processing Blockset FFT block may not match the results from simulation.
Workaround: Change the optimization to a lower setting, such as None or -o1.
